title: Full-Time Podcast Manager
description:
Job Title: Full-Time Podcast and Social Media Manager



Company Overview:

We are a fast-growing agency that empowers entrepreneurs to achieve their goals and enhance their visibility through impactful content creation. Our mission includes producing and managing high-quality podcast content and leveraging social media strategies to engage audiences, build brand authority, and drive growth.



Position Overview:

We are seeking an experienced Podcast and Social Media Manager to oversee and manage multiple podcasts while driving social media marketing efforts. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in podcast production, social media management, guest coordination, and content promotion. This is a full-time role that requires excellent organizational skills, creativity, and the ability to handle the unique needs of various clients.



Key Responsibilities:



Podcast Management:

• Manage all aspects of podcast production for multiple shows, ensuring timely and high-quality delivery.

• Coordinate guest interviews, including outreach, scheduling, and preparing guests for their appearances.

• Develop and manage production schedules, ensuring deadlines are consistently met.

• Create and oversee workflows to streamline podcast operations and optimize efficiency.

• Collaborate with editors and content creators to produce engaging audio and video content.

• Write and edit show notes, titles, and descriptions for each episode.

• Distribute episodes across podcast platforms (e.g., Spotify, Apple Podcasts) and track performance metrics.

• Monitor analytics and provide clients with regular reports on podcast performance.

• Stay updated on podcasting trends, tools, and industry best practices to ensure competitive positioning.



Social Media Management:

• Develop and execute social media strategies to promote podcast episodes and other related content.

• Create and schedule posts on platforms like Instagram, LinkedIn,
, TikTok, and
.

• Edit and repurpose podcast content into engaging social media clips and graphics.

• Use tools like CapCut, Canva, or Descript to optimize and streamline social media content creation.

• Engage with followers, respond to comments, and foster online communities around podcast brands.

• Monitor social media analytics to measure campaign performance and optimize strategies for better engagement and reach.



Additional Responsibilities:

• Coordinate with marketing teams to align podcast and social media strategies with broader client goals.

• Assist in advertising and sponsorship outreach for podcast monetization opportunities.

• Conduct market and competition analysis to identify trends and new opportunities in podcasting and social media.



Qualifications:

• Proven experience managing multiple podcasts or similar content projects.

• Strong understanding of podcast platforms, workflows, and best practices.

• Solid experience in social media management and marketing, including analytics and campaign optimization.

• Familiarity with social media platforms and tools such as Hootsuite, Buffer, or Sprout Social.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills for guest coordination and audience engagement.

• Proficiency in podcast editing software (e.g., Audacity, Adobe Audition) and social media content creation tools.

• Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment while maintaining high attention to detail.



Preferred Qualifications:

• Experience with video podcasting and SEO optimization for podcast episodes and promotional materials.

• Knowledge of automation tools like Zapier and CRM platforms.

• Familiarity with paid social media advertising strategies.



What We Offer:

• Flexible remote working environment.

• Opportunities for professional growth and development.

• Collaborative and supportive team culture.

• Competitive compensation based on experience



Application Requirements:

To apply for this position, candidates MUST complete all three steps below:

1. Submit a resume outlining your experience.

2. Provide a portfolio showcasing podcasts and social media projects you’ve managed.

3. Submit a link to a newly created video introduction where you:

• State the current date (to verify it’s a new video).

• Explain why you are the perfect fit for this role.

• Highlight relevant projects you’ve completed and your approach to podcast management and social media marketing.

• Showcase examples of your work or workflows if possible.



Please Note:

• Do not apply if you do not complete all three requirements as described above.

• Old video introductions or videos that do not include all required components will NOT be considered.

• Applications submitted directly on other platforms will not be considered.



How to Apply:

Submit your application via this link:

title: Digital Marketing and SEO Coordinator
description:
We run a growing home care company focused on helping seniors live safely and independently in their own homes. Our business depends on consistent, measurable results—not trends, not vanity metrics.



We are hiring a full-time SEO & Digital Marketing Specialist to work directly with our internal team.

This is a direct hire position (not an agency role).



You will be part of our long-term operation and expected to execute, improve, and take ownership of results.



If you are looking for short-term work, multiple clients, or passive tasks—this is not a fit.



What You Will Own



Your primary responsibility is simple:



???? Increase qualified inbound phone calls and inquiries



You will do this through:



Website Management & Optimization



Update and improve website content (WordPress or similar)

Improve site speed, mobile performance, and basic UX

Fix technical SEO issues (indexing, structure, broken links)



Local SEO Execution



Keyword research based on intent (calls, not traffic)

On-page optimization and internal linking

Improve rankings that lead to real inquiries



Landing Pages & Conversion



Build and improve service pages for local markets

Optimize pages based on performance (not guesswork)



Google Business Profile Management



Ongoing posts, photos, and updates

Review monitoring and responses

Track calls, clicks, and direction requests



Call-Driven Marketing Support



Improve landing pages and ad performance

Use call tracking data to identify lead quality

Adjust messaging based on real conversations



Reporting & Accountability



Weekly reporting on:

Calls generated

Inquiry volume

SEO performance

GBP activity

Clear breakdown of:

What is working

What is not working

What needs to change

What We Value

You focus on results, not activity

You understand that traffic without conversion is useless

You execute without needing constant supervision

You think in systems and repeatable processes

You are comfortable being measured on real outcomes

Required Experience

WordPress (or similar CMS)

This Role Is NOT For You If

You mainly do social media posting

You rely on backlinks, spam tactics, or shortcuts

You focus on impressions, likes, or traffic alone

You prefer strategy discussions over actual execution

Role Structure

Full-time remote position

Direct hire (no agencies, no outsourcing teams)

Long-term opportunity with growth based on performance

Application Process (Strict)



We are looking for people who follow instructions and can execute.



Step 1 – Initial Application



Send:



A short summary of your experience

At least 2 examples of:

SEO results tied to calls or leads

How would you increase phone calls for a local home care business?

Confirm:

???? You are applying as an individual (not an agency)

Include the word Neptune in your subject line

Step 2 – Paid Test Task



Selected applicants will be invited to complete a paid test task ($10).



This is a short, real-world assignment (1–2 hours max), not busywork.



Example of what you may be asked to do:

Suggest SEO and conversion fixes

Rewrite a section of content

Provide a short, practical action plan



We use this step to evaluate:



How you think

How you execute

Whether you focus on real outcomes (calls, not just traffic)



All test tasks are paid.

We do not request free work.



Final Note



We are building a long-term, stable marketing system.

We are not looking for hacks, shortcuts, or inflated metrics.



If you take ownership, execute consistently, and focus on real results, this role will grow with you.

title: Website, SEO, Google Business Profile & Digital/Phone Marketing
description:
We run a growing home care business that helps seniors live longer, healthier, and more independent lives in their own homes. We’re focused on results, sustainability, and long-term growth—not hype, vanity metrics, or shortcuts.



We’re looking for a skilled Virtual Assistant who understands how websites, SEO, Google Business Profiles, and marketing systems work together to consistently generate qualified inquiries and phone calls.



This is a hands-on execution role, not a strategy-only role.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

Website Management



Maintain and update our website (pages, content, basic UX improvements)



Ensure site speed, mobile optimization, and basic technical SEO hygiene



Implement landing pages or service pages that support local lead generation



SEO (Local & On-Page)



On-page SEO (titles, meta descriptions, headers, internal linking)



Local SEO focused on service areas



Keyword research with intent (calls & inquiries, not traffic for traffic’s sake)



Monitor rankings and make adjustments based on performance



Google Business Profile (GBP)



Optimize and manage Google Business Profile(s)



Weekly posts, photos, and updates



Review monitoring and response support



Track GBP insights and actions (calls, direction requests, clicks)



Digital & Phone Call Marketing



Support digital campaigns designed to generate phone calls, not just clicks



Assist with call tracking setup and basic reporting



Help optimize ads, landing pages, and messaging based on call quality



Coordinate simple follow-ups or workflows tied to inbound calls



Reporting & Accountability



Weekly reporting on:



Website performance



SEO progress



Google Business activity



Calls and inquiries generated



Flag what’s working, what’s not, and what needs fixing



What We’re Looking For



Required Skills & Experience



Proven experience managing:



Websites (WordPress or similar)



Local SEO



Google Business Profiles



Solid understanding of call-driven local marketing



Comfortable using tools like:



Google Search Console



Google Analytics



Call tracking tools



SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar)



Strong attention to detail and follow-through



You’re a Good Fit If You:



Care more about results than buzzwords



Can execute without constant hand-holding



Think in systems, not random tasks



Are comfortable being measured on outcomes



Communicate clearly and honestly when something isn’t working



What This Is NOT



Not a “post on social media and hope” role



Not a branding or influencer role



Not a place for shortcuts, spammy SEO, or fake metrics



Work Details



Remote / Virtual



Part-time to start (with potential to grow)



Flexible hours, outcome-focused



Long-term opportunity for the right person



How to Apply



Please include:



A brief summary of your relevant experience



Examples of websites, SEO work, or Google Business Profiles you’ve managed



One short paragraph explaining how you help local businesses get more phone calls



Finally if you got this far, please add on the title line Gemini for me to get your attention.

title: Homecare Caregiver recruiter
description:
We are a licensed home care agency serving seniors across South Central Minnesota. Our caregivers help older adults remain independent and safe in their own homes. As our service area continues to grow, we are hiring a full-time remote Recruiter who will take ownership of building and maintaining a steady pipeline of qualified caregivers. This role requires someone who understands that recruiting in home care is an operational function, not just a hiring activity. Your responsibility is to ensure the organization always has enough caregivers in the right cities so that seniors who need care can receive it without delay.



The recruiter will manage the full caregiver recruiting cycle, from attracting applicants to moving qualified candidates into the hiring process. You will actively manage job postings, communicate with applicants by phone, screen candidates, and maintain an organized recruiting pipeline. This role also requires close coordination with scheduling, HR, and leadership so that hiring activity matches the real staffing needs of the business. Success in this position means anticipating shortages before they happen and ensuring we always have caregivers available where demand exists.



This role involves a high level of daily communication with applicants and requires strong organization. You will review applicant flow, conduct structured phone screens, follow up with candidates who have incomplete applications, and ensure interviews are scheduled efficiently. You will also track recruiting metrics such as applicant volume, interview conversion, hires, and hiring trends by geographic area. The goal is to keep the recruiting pipeline healthy so the company can continue expanding services without staffing interruptions.



We are looking for someone who has at least two years of recruiting experience and is comfortable handling frequent phone conversations with applicants. Experience recruiting hourly workers, especially in healthcare or home care, is helpful but not required. Strong written and spoken English is essential, along with solid spreadsheet skills and the ability to stay organized while managing multiple applicants at different stages of the process.



The right candidate is reliable, calm under pressure, and focused on getting results. We value people who communicate clearly, take ownership of their work, and approach recruiting with a problem-solving mindset. If you apply for this position, please include the word Apollo somewhere in your response so we know you read the entire posting.

title: Web & UX Designer (Figma + WordPress)  / Contract
description:
Web & UX Designer (Figma + WordPress) –Contract

About Us



We are a performance-driven digital marketing agency focused on building high-converting websites for local businesses in home services and healthcare.



Our work is not just design. It is revenue-focused. Every website must be fast, structured, and built to generate leads.



The Role



We are looking for a Web & UX Designer who can own the entire website process from concept to launch.



This is not a “just design” role. You will be responsible for execution, coordination, and final output quality.



You must be comfortable:



Designing in Figma

Building in WordPress

Managing developers when needed

Ensuring the final product performs (UX, speed, SEO, conversion)

What You’ll Do

Design & Build

Create wireframes, mockups, and full prototypes in Figma

Build responsive WordPress websites based on your designs

Ensure mobile-first and conversion-focused layouts

Project Ownership

Manage full website lifecycle from start to launch

Coordinate with strategists, copywriters, and developers

Keep projects on timeline and within scope

Quality & Performance

Optimize websites for:

UX and clarity

Page speed

SEO structure

Conversion (calls, forms, CTAs)

Client Communication

Present designs and rationale clearly

Collect feedback and manage revisions

Push back when needed to protect quality

What We’re Looking For

Strong portfolio of modern, conversion-focused websites (Figma required)

3+ years building WordPress websites (not just themes, real builds)

Clear understanding of:

Layout hierarchy

User behavior

Conversion principles

Strong communication and project management skills

Ability to work independently and take ownership

Bonus Points

SEO knowledge (site structure, internal linking, schema basics)

CRO experience (landing pages, A/B thinking)

Basic HTML/CSS understanding

Experience working with page builders (Bricks, Elementor, etc.)

SOP & Execution Responsibility (Critical)



You will be responsible for creating and following a structured workflow.



Website Project SOP (Start to Finish)

1. Discovery & Planning

Review intake form and client goals

Define:

Target audience

Core services

Conversion goals

Create sitemap and page structure

2. Wireframing (Figma)

Create low-fidelity wireframes

Focus on structure, not visuals

Validate:

CTA placement

Content flow

User journey

3. High-Fidelity Design

Apply branding, colors, typography

Design full pages (desktop + mobile)

Ensure consistency across all pages

4. Development (WordPress)

Build pages based on approved designs

Use clean structure:

Reusable sections

Proper spacing system

Implement:

Forms

Tracking (basic readiness for GTM)

Speed optimization basics

5. Developer Management (if involved)

Provide clear Figma specs and instructions

Review builds against design (pixel-level check)

Validate:

Responsiveness

Speed

Functionality

Send structured feedback (not vague comments)

6. QA Checklist Before Launch

Mobile responsiveness (all breakpoints)

Page speed (Core Web Vitals baseline)

SEO basics:

Meta titles/descriptions

H1–H3 structure

Internal linking

Forms working and tested

Call tracking / analytics readiness

No broken links

7. Launch

Final review with team

Push live

Monitor first 48 hours for issues

8. Post-Launch Optimization

Identify UX issues (drop-offs, friction points)

Suggest improvements:

CTA placement

Content clarity

Layout adjustments

Iterate based on performance

Why Join Us

Small, focused team with high standards

Real ownership over projects

Opportunity to grow into a lead role

Flexible and remote-first

Work on projects that directly impact client revenue

How to Apply



Send:



Portfolio (must include Figma + live websites)

Resume

Short note explaining:

How you approach a website project from start to finish

Example of a project you owned completely

title: AI Expert – Data Engineer (Remote | Philippines)
description:
Job Title: AI Expert – Data Engineer (Remote | Philippines)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based applicants only)

Employment Type: Full-Time / Contract (Flexible based on experience)

Industry: Healthcare / Telehealth / AI Innovation



About the Role

We are looking for a highly skilled AI Expert – Data Engineer to join our growing team. This role will be responsible for designing, building, and optimizing data pipelines and AI-driven systems to support business intelligence, automation, and advanced analytics across our operations.



You will play a key role in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling smarter decision-making and scalable AI solutions.



Key Responsibilities

- Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and architectures

- Develop and deploy AI/ML models for automation, prediction, and optimization

- Work with large datasets (structured & unstructured) for data processing and analysis

- Integrate AI solutions into existing systems (CRM, telehealth platforms, e-commerce)

- Optimize data workflows for performance, reliability, and cost-efficiency

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Operations, Marketing, Tech)

- Ensure data quality, security, and compliance standards are met

- Stay updated with latest AI, ML, and data engineering trends



Requirements

- Proven experience as a Data Engineer, AI Engineer, or similar role

-Strong proficiency in:

         * Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, etc.)

         * SQL & database management (PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, etc.)

Experience with:

- Machine Learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, etc.)

- Data pipeline tools (Airflow, Spark, Kafka, etc.)

- Cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

- Experience working with APIs and system integrations

- Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL processes, and data warehousing

- Ability to work independently in a remote setup

- Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills



Nice to Have

- Experience with LLMs / Generative AI (OpenAI, LangChain, etc.)

- Experience in healthcare, telehealth, or e-commerce industries

- Knowledge of automation tools and workflow orchestration

- Experience building dashboards (Power BI, Tableau, Looker)



Compensation & Benefits

- Competitive salary (based on experience)

- Remote work flexibility



How to Apply

To apply for this position, please complete the Google Form below. (remove spaces)

Applications submitted through OnlineJobs messages will not be reviewed.

Application Form:  https: // forms. gle / AkLcAC38kwtTthvU8



Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

title: Outbound Enrollment Closer – Career Certification Programs (Remote, EST)
description:
Outbound Enrollment Closer – Career Certification Programs (Remote, EST)



This is not customer service.

This is not inbound order-taking.



This is a phone-driven, outbound enrollment closer role for professionals who know how to control a consult, qualify fast, overcome objections, and close.



We are a U.S.-based online career school enrolling students into IT and Project Management certification programs (CompTIA, PMP, and related tracks). We provide warm leads. You bring the discipline, urgency, and closing skill.



What You’ll Be Doing (Daily Reality)



Make high-volume outbound calls to prospective students



Run structured, consultative enrollment calls (needs analysis ? recommendation ? close)



Control the call, set next steps, and ask for the enrollment



Handle objections around price, time, confidence, and urgency



Follow up relentlessly until the prospect converts or disqualifies



Log activity and outcomes accurately in CRM



Hit daily activity targets and monthly enrollment goals



Improve close rates through coaching, scripts, and call reviews



If you don’t like calling, this role will break you.



Who This Role Is For



Proven outbound / inside sales closers



Comfortable dialing daily and living on the phone



Strong command of English with clear U.S. cultural fluency



Confident asking direct questions and pushing for decisions



Competitive, coachable, metrics-driven



Organized and accountable without supervision



Experience selling education, training, coaching, or professional services is a major plus



Familiarity with certifications (CompTIA, PMP) helps—but closing ability matters more



Bachelor’s degree required



Who Should NOT Apply



Anyone who needs inbound-only leads



Anyone uncomfortable asking for money



Anyone who avoids objection handling



Anyone who wants a “soft” admissions role



Anyone who disappears when targets tighten



Schedule



Monday–Friday



10:00 AM – 6:30 PM EST



Compensation



Base pay: $1,200–$1,600 USD per month



Performance-based advancement and leadership path



Long-term role with stability and growth for performers



(Top performers move fast. Average performers don’t last.)



What You Get



Warm leads with real intent



Clear scripts, frameworks, and coaching



No gimmicks, no fake promises—just real programs that work



A sales culture that rewards results



Bottom Line



If you can:



Control a sales conversation



Build urgency without lying



Close consistently



Take coaching and improve



You will win here.



If not—this role will expose you quickly.

title: Social Media Lead Generation Specialist
description:
Full-Time | Remote | $1,000–$1,600 + Performance Bonuses



We are a U.S.-based online education company that enrolls students into professional certification programs such as IT certifications, cybersecurity, and project management (PMP).



We are looking for a high-performing Social Media Lead Generation Specialist who knows how to turn social media attention into real prospects and scheduled calls with our admissions team.



This role is not just posting content. Your success will be measured by appointments generated and prospects who show up to speak with our team.



If you are a marketer who understands funnels, engagement, and lead generation, this can be a long-term opportunity with a growing U.S. company.



What You Will Be Responsible For



Your primary goal is to generate qualified prospects through social media and convert them into booked calls with our admissions team.



Daily responsibilities include:



• Creating and scheduling engaging social media content

• Generating inbound leads through organic marketing

• Managing comments, messages, and inquiries from prospects

• Converting social media interest into scheduled admissions calls

• Running outreach campaigns and engagement strategies

• Creating short-form content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts)

• Monitoring lead activity and reporting daily results

• Working closely with our sales team to coordinate booked calls



Platforms You Will Work With



•

• Instagram

• TikTok

• LinkedIn

• YouTube Shorts



Experience with paid social advertising is a plus but not required.



Ideal Candidate



We are looking for someone who:



• Has real experience generating leads from social media

• Understands marketing funnels and audience targeting

• Is reliable, disciplined, and results-driven

• Communicates clearly in English

• Is proactive and brings new marketing ideas

• Is comfortable working with a U.S. team



Bonus if you have experience marketing:



• Online courses

• Education programs

• Certification training

• Professional services



Required Skills



• Social media marketing

• Lead generation strategy

• Content creation

• Basic analytics and reporting

• Copywriting for engagement and conversion



Helpful tools (not required):



• Canva

• CapCut

• Meta Business Suite

• CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.)

• Zapier or marketing automation tools



Compensation



Base Salary:

$1,000 – $1,600 per month depending on experience.



Performance Bonus:

$10 for every qualified appointment that shows up for a scheduled call with our admissions team.



A strong performer can earn significant monthly bonuses by consistently generating booked calls.



Work Schedule



Full-time position

40 hours per week

Remote



Must be dependable and responsive during scheduled working hours.



To Apply



Please include the following in your application:



Links to social media accounts or campaigns you have managed



A brief explanation of how you generate leads from social media



A short 1–2 minute video introduction



To confirm you read the full job description, include the word “ENROLLMENT” at the beginning of your application.



Applications without examples will not be considered.

title: Sales Manager – Student Enrollment Group
description:
Team Lead – Student Enrollment Group



U.S. Online Career School – Medical, IT & Project Management Programs



Are you an experienced enrollment/sales professional ready to guide and support a high-performing student enrollment support team? Do you have exceptional English communication skills and strong familiarity with U.S. culture?



Join a leading U.S.-based online career school that empowers students to build successful careers in Information Technology and Project Management. We are seeking a Team Lead for our Sales Assistant Group to provide daily oversight, maintain communication standards, and ensure a professional, compliant, and student-centered enrollment experience.



This is a hands-on leadership role within a structured, performance-focused remote environment serving U.S.-based students.



Key Responsibilities

Team Guidance & Oversight



• Provide daily direction and support to Sales Assistants

• Monitor activity levels, follow-ups, and enrollment progress

• Ensure adherence to enrollment processes and institutional standards

• Maintain high communication quality in all student interactions



Coaching & Quality Control



• Conduct call reviews and provide structured feedback

• Reinforce best practices in consultation, documentation, and professionalism

• Assist with onboarding and training new Sales Assistants

• Support team accountability and workflow consistency



Operational Support



• Coordinate with admissions, compliance, and academic teams

• Ensure enrollment documentation is accurate and complete

• Help identify process improvements to increase efficiency

• Step in during high-volume periods or escalated situations



Direct Enrollment Contribution



• Assist with consultations when needed

• Support lead management and follow-up consistency

• Maintain knowledge of IT and business certifications (e.g., CompTIA, PMP)



Requirements



• Proven experience in sales, admissions, or enrollment support

• Prior team lead or informal leadership experience preferred

• Strong English communication skills are required (both written and verbal)

• Ability to communicate clearly, confidently, and professionally with U.S.-based students

• Strong familiarity with U.S. culture and communication standards

• Demonstrated ability to meet structured enrollment targets

• Strong organizational and leadership skills

• Comfortable working in a remote team environment

• Familiarity with IT or business certifications is a strong plus

• Bachelor’s degree required

• Availability Monday to Friday, 10:00 AM – 6:30 PM EST



Compensation & Benefits



• Fixed Salary: $1,200 – $1,800 USD per month (based on experience)

• 100% remote work

• Professional growth opportunities within the U.S. education sector

• Structured leadership responsibilities

• Supportive and collaborative team culture

title: Social Media Manager — US Mortgage & Real Estate Brand | Remote
description:
COMPANY: One of the Fastest-Growing Mortgage Brokers in the United States

LOCATION: 100% Remote — Work from Home 

SCHEDULE: Full-Time | Monday–Friday | Flexible hours with PST overlap (minimum 4 hours overlap required)

COMPENSATION: PHP 30,000–55,000/month depending on experience

TYPE: Full-Time | Long-Term | Immediate Start



---



ABOUT THE COMPANY



We are one of the fastest-growing mortgage brokerage brands in the United States. We close home loans, HELOCs, DSCR investor loans, refinances, and non-QM products across California, Texas, Florida, and beyond. Our brand is built online — Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and
 — and we are growing fast.



We are hiring a dedicated Social Media Manager to own our digital presence, grow our audience, and turn followers into leads and referral partners. This is not a task execution role. This is a brand-building role.



---



ABOUT THE ROLE



You will be the person behind our social media accounts every single day. You plan the content calendar, write the captions, schedule the posts, engage with our community, respond to DMs and comments, track what's working, and bring new ideas to the table every week.



You won't be editing videos — we have a dedicated video editor for that. Your job is everything else: strategy, posting, engagement, growth, and reporting.



If you've built or managed social media accounts that actually grew — not just existed — this role is for you.



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Content & Posting

- Manage and maintain a weekly content calendar across Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and
 etc.

- Schedule and publish all content consistently — no missed posting days

- Write captions that sound human, relatable, and on-brand — not corporate or AI-generated

- Coordinate with our video editor to receive finished content and get it posted on time

- Repurpose content across platforms to maximize every piece

- Source and use trending audio, hooks, and formats to boost reach



Community & Engagement

- Respond to every comment and DM within 24 hours — faster on high-priority accounts

- Actively engage with our audience, referral partners, and target community daily

- Build genuine relationships in the mortgage, real estate, and personal finance space online

- Monitor brand mentions and conversations and flag anything important



Growth & Strategy

- Research trending topics and content angles in mortgage, real estate, and personal finance

- Pitch 3–5 new content ideas weekly with hooks, formats, and goals

- Identify collaboration and partnership opportunities with realtors, investors, and influencers

- Help grow our following organically — real people, real engagement, no bots



Reporting & Analytics

- Track weekly metrics: follower growth, reach, engagement rate, profile visits, DM volume

- Provide a clean weekly report with wins, learnings, and recommendations

- Identify what content is performing and double down — identify what isn't and adjust



---



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Required:

- 2+ years of social media management experience — you have real accounts with real growth to show

- Strong English writing skills — captions, DMs, and responses must be natural and professional

- Deep understanding of Instagram and TikTok algorithms and what drives organic growth

- Organized and consistent — you don't miss posting days or let DMs go unread

- Comfortable working independently and managing your own time

- Familiar with scheduling tools (GHL, Buffer, Meta Business Suite, or similar)

- Reliable internet connection with a backup plan



Strong Bonus:

- Experience in mortgage, real estate, finance, or any regulated industry

- Experience growing a brand account from low following to 10K+

- Canva skills for creating simple graphics, story templates, or captions

- Basic understanding of Meta Ads or paid social (not required but a huge plus)

- Bilingual English/Spanish — our audience is heavily CA/TX/FL

- Experience managing YouTube community tab, Shorts, or long-form scheduling



Not a Fit If:

- You've only managed personal accounts or accounts with no measurable growth

- You need daily instructions on what to post

- Your writing sounds robotic, templated, or AI-generated

- You can't commit to consistent daily engagement

- You have no backup for internet or power outages



---



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS



Starting Pay: PHP 30,000–55,000/month based on experience

Performance Bonus: Monthly bonus tied to follower growth and engagement milestones

Paid Training: Full onboarding on our brand voice, tone, and content strategy

Long-Term Role: We are not looking for a contractor — we want someone who grows with us

Career Growth: Top performer path to Head of Marketing as the brand scales

Flexible Schedule: Results matter more than hours — we trust you to manage your time

PTO after probationary period

US Holidays + select Philippine holidays observed



---



WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT



- You work directly with the founder — your ideas get heard and acted on immediately

- Real brand, real loan volume, real audience to build

- No agency middleman — direct hire, stable position

- Mortgage and real estate content is one of the fastest-growing niches on social media right now

- You'll have a dedicated video editor already on the team — your job is strategy and execution, not production

- Ground floor opportunity on a team scaling toward $10M+/month in loan volume

- Your work directly helps American families buy homes and build wealth — it means something



---



HOW TO APPLY



Step 1: Complete our short personality and work style assessment (5–8 minutes). This is required for all applicants — no exceptions:



???? 
lify.app



Step 2:
 your application to 



Subject line: Social Media Manager PH — [Your Name]



In your
 include:

- Links to 2–3 social media accounts you've managed (Instagram, TikTok,
, or YouTube)

- Your biggest win — one account or campaign you're most proud of and what results you drove

- A brief answer (3–5 sentences) to this question: Looking at our niche (mortgage/real estate in the US), what content angle or strategy would you focus on first to grow our Instagram and why?

- Your available start date

- Your internet speed and backup solution for outages



Applications without the personality assessment and account examples will not be reviewed.



---



IMPORTANT NOTES



- This is a direct hire — no agency, no recruiter fees.

- We require a minimum 4-hour daily overlap with PST business hours. Please only apply if you can commit to this.

- We are building something real and long-term. We want someone who wants to grow with us — not just fill a role.

- A short Loom or video introduction showing us your personality is a strong bonus.

title: Company: Perfectly Fast Moving – Long Distance Moving Carrier
description:
About Us:

Perfectly Fast Moving is a full-service long-distance moving company based in the U.S. Unlike brokers, we handle everything with our own trucks and crews. We are expanding our remote team and hiring motivated individuals to join us as Customer Sales Representatives. All we do is scam people so if you want to work and not get paid this is the place to be.



Job Description:

You will begin as a call opener, engaging with potential customers and collecting their moving details. With training and strong performance, you will grow into a closer—handling full sales calls and securing bookings. This role offers a clear career path and high earning potential.



Responsibilities:



Answer inbound calls and make outbound follow-ups.



Gather customer information and moving details.



Provide excellent customer service with fluent, confident English communication.



Transition into a closer role to handle complete sales calls and secure bookings.



Work with a team to meet company goals and daily call targets.



Qualifications:



Fluent in spoken and written English (clear and natural accent required).



Strong communication and listening skills.



Sales background preferred but not required (training provided).



Must have a reliable computer, stable internet connection, and quiet workspace.



Motivated, coachable, and eager to grow into a closer role.



Work Schedule (Eastern Time):



Shift 1: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (1-hour lunch break)



Shift 2: 3:00 PM – 12:00 Midnight (1-hour lunch break)



Compensation & Benefits:



Base Pay: PHP 31,000 monthly



Commission: 5% per sale – top performers can earn up to PHP 240,000 monthly total (base + commissions)



PLEASE SUBMIT A VOICE RECORIDNG EXPLAINING YOUR WORK AND SALES EXPERIENCE, YOUR CURRENT WORK STATUS AND AVAILABILITY.



Daily training and support provided via Zoom



Referral program: We are hiring 50 people in total – if you have fluent English-speaking friends, you are encouraged to refer them

title: Video Editor
description:
Video Editor (Real Estate Content -- Long & Short Form)



I’m a real estate agent based in Charlotte, North Carolina, and I create a high volume of content across YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram.



I’m looking for a reliable, detail-oriented video editor to help me produce both long-form and short-form videos on a consistent basis.



This is NOT heavy editing -- no complex effects, animations, or advanced graphics.

However, this role requires extreme attention to detail, especially when it comes to visuals.



What You’ll Be Doing

Edit talking-head videos (cutting, smoothing, pacing)

Add basic captions/subtitles

Insert relevant images and visuals to support what I’m talking about

Ensure videos feel clean, natural, and engaging

Maintain consistent editing style across all content



Most Important Skill (Read Carefully)



A huge part of this role is finding the RIGHT images to match what I’m saying.



You must be able to search online and find accurate, high-quality images

The images must match the exact location, building, or topic being discussed

Using the wrong visuals is a major mistake in this role



Example: If I’m talking about a specific neighborhood or development in Charlotte, you must find the correct, real images of that exact place -- not something similar.



This is the #1 priority for this position.



Requirements

Strong English comprehension (you must clearly understand what I’m saying)

Ability to follow instructions closely

Strong research skills (finding accurate visuals quickly)

Basic video editing skills (cutting, timing, captions)

Reliable and consistent communication

Experience with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or similar tools



Nice to Have (Not Required)

Experience editing real estate content

Familiarity with U.S. cities (especially Charlotte is a plus)

Understanding of short-form content pacing (TikTok/Reels)



Work Style

Ongoing work (multiple videos per week)

Simple editing workflow -- designed for speed and consistency

Clear direction and feedback provided



To Apply

Please include:



Examples of videos you’ve edited (especially talking-head or simple edits)

Your editing software

The secret word "watermelon" to see who's paying attention

title: PAID OPPORTUNITY: Graphic Designer for High-Converting E-commerce Ads (DTC / Paid Social)
description:
We’re looking for a talented graphic designer to create high-converting static ads for a fast-growing DTC skincare brand.



This role is focused on designing scroll-stopping creatives for paid social platforms like Meta and TikTok, so an understanding of performance-driven design is important. You should have a strong eye for clean, modern design and know how to balance aesthetics with conversion.



You’ll be creating static ad creatives, iterating on winning designs, and developing new concepts based on performance insights. We’re looking for someone who can move quickly, test ideas, and continuously improve creative output.



Ideally, you have experience working with e-commerce brands and can show a portfolio of ad creatives, not just branding work. You understand what makes an ad convert, from hooks to layout to visual hierarchy, and can design with intention.



We’re building a brand focused on simplicity, clarity, and real results, and we want creatives that reflect that.



To be considered, please send your portfolio along with 2–3 ad creatives you’ve made that performed well.

testing and learning

title: SEO Implementer
description:
SEO Implementer

Full-Time (40 hrs/week) | $400–$600/month | Long-Term Position

About Us

We're a small, family-run holistic pet supplement company that has been helping dogs live healthier lives for over 25 years. We specialize in herbal supplement programs for dogs with kidney, heart, and liver health issues.

We're good people looking for other good people. We treat our team with respect, we communicate openly, and we're looking for someone who wants to be a true member of the team long-term. SEO is a long game — we know that. We're not looking for quick fixes or someone who's going to disappear after a few months. We want someone who's reliable, takes pride in their work, and is excited to grow with us.

The Role

This is not a "do whatever you think is best" SEO position. We have a detailed, step-by-step professional SEO course that serves as your training manual and playbook. Your first task will be to watch the entire course and understand the methodology before touching anything on the sites. After you've completed the course, you'll map out your plan based on what you learned. Then we'll go over it together, discuss tools and software, and get everything set up before work begins.

We don't need you to have years of SEO experience. We need you to be a fast, thorough learner who can follow a proven system precisely and execute it consistently. If you're the kind of person who takes detailed notes when watching training videos, asks smart questions, and takes pride in following a process correctly — this role is built for you.

What the Course Covers

The SEO course you'll be trained on is a professional, 38-video program covering a complete bottom-of-funnel SEO methodology. Here's what you'll learn:



Technical SEO and site audits (crawlability, page speed, indexing, sitemaps)

Bottom-of-funnel keyword research — finding low-competition, high-intent keywords that larger competitors overlook

How to manually analyze search engine results pages (SERPs) to evaluate real competition — not just relying on difficulty scores

Building SEO landing pages on WordPress following a specific on-page structure

Scoring and optimizing every page using professional SEO tools

Site architecture, hub pages, and internal linking strategy

White hat link building — directories, expert quotes, press releases, and launch platforms

Video SEO fundamentals



Everything is taught step by step with templates, checklists, and real examples. You'll have a clear system to follow from day one.

Your Day-to-Day Work

Page Creation (Core of the Job)



Building bottom-of-funnel SEO landing pages on WordPress using Elementor, 3–5 new pages per week

Following the course's specific on-page SEO structure for every page (keyword placement in URL, page title, meta description, H1, first sentence)

Each page is roughly 400–500 words. Writing must sound natural and authentic — reflecting our brand voice and our founder's 25 years of expertise

Using AI tools (Claude or ChatGPT) to help draft content, then editing to match our brand voice. We'll provide brand voice guidelines and writing examples

Scoring and optimizing every page using the tools taught in the course

Submitting every new page to Google Search Console and tracking keyword rankings weekly



Keyword Research



Finding bottom-of-funnel keywords in the pet supplement and dog health niche

Manually analyzing SERPs to evaluate real competition using the course methodology

Building and maintaining a master keyword research spreadsheet



Technical SEO & Site Audits



Running site audits to identify and fix technical issues

Ensuring all sites meet technical SEO best practices before building new pages



Site Architecture & Internal Linking



Creating hub pages that organize landing pages by category

Managing internal linking so Google understands the site structure



Link Building



Submitting our sites to relevant directories (pet health, holistic supplements, natural pet care)

Submitting to launch platforms (Product Hunt, BetaList) for product launches

Responding to expert quote requests from journalists on our founder's behalf — she has 25 years of expertise, perfect for this

Press releases for product launches and company milestones

Tracking all link building activity in a spreadsheet



QA, Optimization & Reporting



Running the QA process taught in the course on every published page

Diagnosing and improving underperforming pages using the course's troubleshooting methodology

Keeping a change log for every modification made to any page

Providing a weekly update: what you worked on, what's published, what's in progress, and any issues or questions. We do weekly check-ins and we'll set up a simple reporting format together



Tools & Software (We Provide Everything)

You don't need to own any of these — we provide all subscriptions and licenses. The course teaches you how to use each one:



Moz Pro — keyword research, SERP analysis, On-Page Grader, rank tracking

Screaming Frog — site audits

WordPress + WooCommerce with Elementor Pro — page building and landing pages

Yoast SEO or Rank Math — on-page SEO plugin (we'll d
 together after you've seen the course)

Google Search Console and Google Sheets

TinyPNG, Redirection, Title and Nofollow for Links — WordPress plugins

MozBar, SEO Meta in 1 Click, Detailed SEO Extension — browser extensions

Claude or ChatGPT — for content drafting, keyword brainstorming, grammar checking, and maintaining brand voice



What We're Looking For

You don't need to be an SEO expert. We're hiring for learning ability, process discipline, and strong English writing — not years of SEO experience. The course teaches you the SEO methodology. Here's what you do need:



WordPress — comfortable building and managing WordPress sites

Elementor — ideally you can build pages from scratch in Elementor. If you're familiar with Elementor but haven't built from scratch, that's okay — we can work with templates. But someone who can build freely in Elementor is preferred

Google Sheets — formulas, data organization, filtering

AI tools — you currently use ChatGPT, Claude, or similar LLMs for work. Not just "I've tried it once" — you actively use AI to draft content, brainstorm, check grammar, or research. Preference for Claude experience

Strong English writing — you will be writing 400–500 word landing pages that need to read naturally and sound human. We'll ask for a writing sample

Ability to learn from video courses — this is critical. Your first weeks will be watching a 38-video course, taking notes, and building a plan. If you're not the kind of person who learns well from structured video training, this role isn't for you

Detail-oriented and process-driven — this role is about following a proven system carefully, not improvising your own approach



What We Provide



Access to the full professional SEO course (your training manual — you will complete this before starting any work)

All software subscriptions and plugin licenses listed above

Brand voice guidelines and writing examples so you can match our founder's tone

Direct communication with management

A collaborative, respectful working environment



Your Focus

Your job is SEO and WordPress. We have a separate tea
mber who handles video editing, video creation, and social media management. You don't need to worry about any of that. If your SEO work creates opportunities for video or social content, we'll coordinate between you and our social team.

You also don't need prior SEO experience. If you've never used Moz or done keyword research before, that's fine — the course teaches all of it. What matters is that you can learn it and execute it precisely.

Schedule & Compensation



Full-time: 40 hours/week, Monday to Friday

$400–$600/month depending on experience and skills

Long-term position — we're building something here and we want you to be part of it for the long haul. The SEO course is phase one. As the strategy matures and results come in, we'll expand into broader SEO work together



How to Apply

Please include in your application:



Your experience with WordPress and Elementor — specific examples of sites or pages you've built.

How do you currently use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) in your work? Give us a specific example.

Describe a time you followed a detailed process, training program, or course step by step. What was it, and what was the result?

Type your favorite dog breed at the very top of your application.

Are you comfortable watching a full SEO course (38 videos) and following its methodology step by step, rather than doing things your own way?

A short writing sample (2–3 paragraphs) on any topic you choose. We want to see your natural English writing voice.



We're looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and excited to learn a proven SEO system. If you have solid WordPress skills, you thrive when given a clear structure to follow, and you're looking for a team that treats you well and values long-term commitment — we want to hear from you.

title: Online Math/Science Teacher(Near-Native Accent)
description:
About Pomelo Academy

Pomelo Academy is an innovative online international school dedicated to delivering high-quality education to students worldwide. We provide a dynamic and engaging learning environment, catering to individualized instruction that transcends geographical boundaries.



Job Description

We are seeking passionate and experienced Near-Native English-speaking Online K-12 Math and/or Science Teachers with verifiable teaching certifications. The ideal candidates will have strong academic backgrounds in Mathematics and/or Science, experience teaching young learners (ages 5–18), and a commitment to creating a supportive, inquiry-based, and engaging online learning environment.



Responsibilities



Deliver interactive and engaging Math and/or Science lessons to individuals and small groups of 2–6 students aged 5–18.

Teach using internationally recognized curricula such as A Closer Look, Glencoe Science, Pearson Interactive Science, NGSS-aligned resources, Singapore Math, or IB/AP/A Level Math programs.

Create age-appropriate lesson plans focused on conceptual understanding, inquiry, problem-solving, and real-world applications.

Incorporate visuals, multimedia, and hands-on virtual activities to make abstract concepts accessible and engaging.

Monitor student progress and provide timely feedback to students and parents.

Foster curiosity, discussion, and active learning in a safe and supportive space.



Qualifications



Professional teacher with at least 2 years of teaching experience (Kindergarten–Grade 12).

Must hold a valid teaching license/certification (e.g., Teaching License, State Certification). TESOL/TEFL is a plus.

Near-native English accent with excellent spoken and written communication skills.

Strong academic background in Mathematics and/or Science (Biology, Physics, Chemistry, General Science).

Experience teaching in public/international schools or reputable online platforms.

Familiarity with inquiry-based and/or interdisciplinary approaches (e.g., STEM/STEAM).

Comfortable working between 07:00–22:00 (Weekdays) and 9:00–22:00 (Weekends), GMT+8.

Proficiency with digital platforms such as ClassIn, Microsoft Office, and other educational technologies.

Must have a stable and fast internet connection and a PC/laptop with at least Windows 10 or Mac OS Catalina.



Benefits



Flexible working hours.

Opportunity to teach using globally respected Math and Science curricula.

Be part of an international, passionate team of educators.

Access to training and professional development.

Regular enrollment model (not a booking system), ensuring schedule stability and consistency

title: Customer Support Coordinator — Permanent Lighting Company (US-Based) $5/hr + Performance Bonuses
description:
About the Company

We install premium permanent outdoor LED lighting systems on homes across the United States. Think custom-color Christmas lights that stay up year-round — except high-end, professionally installed, and built to last a lifetime. Our customers pay $3,000–$10,000 for their systems and expect a premium experience from start to finish. We're a fast-growing company and we take care of the people on our team.



What You'll Be Doing

This role has two main responsibilities:

1. Roof Measurements (approx. 2–3 hours per morning/afternoon)



Using EagleView and RoofSnap (we'll train you), pull roof measurements for upcoming sales appointments

Calculate linear footage per side of the home using a simple formula we'll teach you

Enter measurements into our CRM (HighLevel) before each appointment's start time — this is time-sensitive and directly tied to your bonus

Handle edge cases like new builds and late appointment confirmations

Flag anything that doesn't look right rather than guessing



2. Customer Inbound Support (remaining hours)



Respond to customer messages in our CRM via text and

Answer inbound phone calls from customers asking about their installation timeline, materials status, or general questions

Look up project status in our systems and give clear, accurate updates

De-escalate frustrated customers calmly and professionally — we'll give you scripts and context

Log notes and escalate anything you can't resolve



This is detail-oriented, process-driven work. We have documented SOPs for everything. You won't be figuring things out on your own — you'll be following a clear system and flagging when something falls outside of it.



Compensation

Base pay: $5.00/hr

Performance bonuses — paid:



Measurements Deadline Bonus: You earn a bonus for every appointment where measurements are uploaded before the appointment start time. Miss the deadline, no bonus. 

Call Answer Rate Bonus: You earn a bonus based on the percentage of inbound customer calls you answer during your shift. We track this.  Targets and bonus amounts will be shared during the interview.



We built the bonus structure this way intentionally — if you're reliable and consistent, you make meaningfully more than the base rate. If you're not, the base is what you get. We want someone who sees the bonus as the norm, not the exception.



You're a Great Fit If You:



Have experience in customer support (phone and chat) — 2+ years preferred

Are extremely reliable and show up on time, every day

Communicate clearly in written and spoken English

Can follow a multi-step process accurately without skipping steps

Are comfortable learning new software tools (we'll train you — but you need to be a fast learner)

Have a stable internet connection and a quiet place to take calls

Are available to start your shift by 6:00 a.m. EST





You're Not a Fit If You:



Need constant supervision to complete routine tasks

Have unreliable internet or frequent power outages with no backup

Are looking for a short-term gig — we want someone long-term

Skip instructions in job applications (more on that below)





The Details



Pay: $5/hr base + performance bonuses

Hours: Part-time to start (~20 hrs/week), with the opportunity to grow to full-time as you prove yourself

Schedule: Monday–Sunday, morning shift aligned to US Central/Eastern time

Equipment: You must have your own computer and reliable internet. A headset for calls is required.

Start date: As soon as the right person is found





How to Apply

We receive a lot of applications. To make sure we find the right person, we ask everyone to complete a short Google Form before we consider your application. It takes about 5 minutes.

Do not send a message here asking for more information. Do not send a resume attachment. Only applications submitted through the form will be reviewed.



Click the link below to apply:





If you can follow that one simple instruction, you're already ahead of most applicants.

title: Controller (US e-commerce)
description:
Title: Controller



Looking for a disciplined financial owner who brings calm to complexity 



The Controller role at FS is for someone who enjoys applying knowledge, logic, and structure to create reliable financial systems. This is not a CFO or advisory role. It is a role for a financial professional who takes ownership of execution and finds satisfaction in predictable, trusted outcomes. 



When this role is done well, leadership stops double-checking numbers, inventory decisions are disciplined, and finance becomes boring in the best possible way. 



If you prefer facts over narratives, systems over heroics, and clarity over urgency, this role is built for you.



The mission 

Your mission is to own the financial operating system. You ensure financials are accurate, 

timely, and decision-ready, with clear cash visibility and enforced controls, without requiring 

constant leadership involvement. 



What you will do 

? Own monthly close and deliver accurate financials on a consistent schedule. 

? Ensure all accounts are reconciled and audit-ready. 

? Maintain clear tracking of owner expenses. 

? Prepare board-ready financial summaries with variances and cash implications. 

? Build and maintain a rolling 13-week cash flow forecast. 

? Surface liquidity risks and tradeoffs early. 

? Own inventory finance and open-to-buy controls. 

? Review open-to-buy monthly with Supply Chain and approve or block purchasing. 

? Design and enforce financial controls and approval workflows. 

? Own AR and AP systems and performance. 

? Direct and review the work of the Accountant. 

? Improve AR procedures to support cash timing. 

? Coordinate sales tax obligations and R&D tax tracking. 

? Audit key vendor invoices and charges as needed. 



What success looks like 

? Financials are delivered on time, every month. 

? Cash visibility is clear, current, and trusted. 

? Inventory decisions reflect cash discipline. 

? Controls prevent errors and surprises. 

? Finance operates calmly and predictably. 

? Leadership trusts the numbers without rechecking. 



This role is for you if 

? You enjoy structured, analytical work. 

? You prefer enforcing standards over negotiating them. 

? You like applying proven methods rather than inventing new ones. 

? You are comfortable owning deadlines and saying no when needed. 

? You communicate in a direct, factual way. 



This role is not for you if 

? You want a strategy-first or advisory role. 

? You dislike routine or repetition. 

? You prefer autonomy without guardrails. 

? You thrive on urgency, ambiguity, or constant change.



To Apply: send cv/resume and answers to the ff questions below:

1) Do you have 5+ years of hands-on accounting/controller experience owning the monthly close process?

If yes, briefly explain what you personally owned end-to-end.



2) Have you worked in a product-based business such as e-commerce, retail, wholesale, inventory-heavy, or consumer goods? If yes, name the company/industry and describe the finance work you handled.



3) Do you have experience with inventory finance, open-to-buy planning, or approving/blocking purchasing decisions based on cash and inventory position?



4) Have you led, reviewed, or managed the work of an Accountant or junior accounting staff? If yes, how many people did you oversee, and what work did you review?



5) Have you worked with U.S. accounting operations, including areas such as sales tax, accruals, month-end close, board reporting, or cash planning? If yes, explain which areas you handled.



6) Are you fully available to work CST hours for this role?

title: (Full Time) SEO Web Auditor
description:

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, delivers high-quality results on time, and follows SEO best practices.

Fixing 3xx redirects

Compressing and resizing images

Resolving duplicate content issues

Identifying and fixing schema markup issues

Handling other technical SEO tasks as required

5. Ability to communicate complex SEO concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

6. A proactive, results-driven mindset.

7. Preferably has experience with e-commerce or large-scale website audits.

8. Familiarity with CMS platforms such as WordPress, Shopify, BigCommerce, Magento, or others.

Apply here:

title: Recruiter
description:
Position Overview



Applied Home Health Network (AHHN) is seeking a results-driven Home Care Recruiter to support our rapid growth across Illinois. This role is responsible for sourcing, screening, and onboarding high-quality caregivers (CNAs, HHAs, RNs, LPNs) to ensure uninterrupted care for our clients.



The ideal candidate is metrics-driven, highly organized, and thrives in a fast-paced healthcare environment, with a strong ability to build pipelines and fill cases quickly and consistently.



Key Responsibilities

1. Full-Cycle Recruitment

Source, screen, and hire caregivers (CNAs, HHAs, RNs, LPNs)

Conduct phone and virtual interviews to assess qualifications and cultural fit

Maintain a consistent pipeline of qualified candidates to meet staffing demands



2. Staffing & Fill Rate Execution

Partner with scheduling and care support teams to fill open shifts and new cases

Prioritize urgent and high-need cases to minimize unassigned hours

Ensure caregivers are matched appropriately based on skills, availability, and location



3. Candidate Sourcing Strategy

Utilize job boards (Indeed, social media, referrals, community outreach)

Develop relationships with:

CNA training schools

Nursing programs

Community organizations

Execute creative sourcing strategies to attract top talent



4. Onboarding & Compliance

Ensure all candidates meet Illinois requirements, including:

Background checks

Certifications and credentials

Health screenings

Coordinate onboarding process and ensure fast time-to-start



5. Data Tracking & Reporting

Track and report on key recruitment metrics:

Time-to-fill

Fill rate

Pipeline volume

Offer acceptance rate

Maintain accurate records in applicant tracking systems



6. Retention Support

Support caregiver engagement initiatives to reduce turnover

Conduct follow-ups with new hires to ensure satisfaction and retention

Collaborate with HR and operations to address staffing challenges

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Fill rate of open shifts (Target: 95%+)

Time-to-fill new cases (Target: ? 5 days)

Reduction in unassigned hours

Number of active caregivers onboarded weekly/monthly

Caregiver retention (30/60/90-day)

title: Short & Long Form Football Content Writer
description:
Hello, my name is Hayes Canupp. I am the founder at JerseyBird, a sportswear company. (
)



We are hiring a writer to help write short and long form scripts for our social media channels. The main topic of the writing will be football (soccer) related, specifically with a focus on kits and kit design.



To view the type of content we currently write, check out our IG reels:







Job Description



This role is focused on turning real-world football kit news, releases, and storylines into high-performing content.



You will research current kit drops, trends, and narratives, extract strong angles, and turn them into scripts for both short-form (TikTok/Reels) and long-form (YouTube) content. The goal is to consistently produce engaging, fast-paced scripts that hold attention and translate well on camera.



You will work closely with our team. A typical workflow is:

 • Identify or receive a content idea (new kit, trend, or storyline)

 • Collaborate with a designer to create a visual concept

 • Write the script that brings the idea to life



This is a high-output role where speed, clarity, and content instinct matter more than perfection.



Responsibilities



 • Research football kit news, releases, and trends daily

 • Identify strong hooks, angles, and storylines

 • Write short-form scripts optimized for retention and engagement

 • Write long-form YouTube scripts with clear structure and narrative flow

 • Adapt tone and pacing depending on platform (short vs long form)

 • Collaborate with designers and iterate based on visuals

 • Deliver scripts quickly and consistently



Requirements



 • Deep knowledge of football and football culture (required)

 • Strong understanding of football kits, kit history, and design trends

 • Proven ability to write engaging short-form and long-form content

 • Ability to find compelling angles from basic information

 • Strong sense of pacing, hooks, and viewer retention

 • Fast turnaround and ability to handle consistent output

 • Fluent English with clear, concise writing



Preferred (Not Required)



 • Experience writing for sports or football content

 • Familiarity with YouTube storytelling formats. Please include the word “Bluebird” in your application title to show you read this full description.

 • Understanding of kit design details (patterns, materials, history)

 • Experience working with content teams



Output Expectations



 • 4-5 short-form scripts per week

 • 1–3 long-form scripts per week

 • Scripts that are clear, structured, and ready to record with minimal revisions



If this fits your skillset, please include examples of past writing or content you’ve worked on when applying.

title: Part-Time Assistant – Social Media (Nomad Cruise)
description:
Join the team behind Nomad Cruise, a floating conference that brings 300+ digital nomads together for community, learning, and adventure across the world's oceans.



About us: 

Nomad Cruise is more than an event - it's a global community. We host immersive cruises that combine inspiring talks, workshops, and unforgettable on-shore adventures, creating deep connections between remote professionals from all over the world. Our core values are community, adventure, and inspiration, and everything we do is designed to reflect them.



The role:

We're looking for an assistant for social media (mainly), project and event. Someone who genuinely enjoys the craft of social media- someone who understands tone, and keeps things consistent. You'll be the voice behind our channels day-to-day, helping us stay active, engaged, and on-brand.



What you'll do:

Video & Content Editing

Edit short-form video content (Reels, Stories, clips) and assist with graphics or visual assets - taking raw material from the team and turning it into polished, publish-ready content.



Community Management

Respond to comments, DMs, and mentions across Instagram and other platforms - keeping our community warm, active, and engaged in a tone that feels human and on-brand.



Content Scheduling & Coordination

Help plan and schedule posts using our content calendar, coordinating with the team to keep channels consistent and active.



Caption Writing & Ideation

Write captions, suggest content ideas, and repurpose existing material to keep our feeds fresh and our voice consistent.



The Details:

Part-time, remote

5–15 hours per week

Flexible schedule, weekends included

Long-term opportunity - with the right fit, we're looking for someone to grow with us



Why you'll love it:

Work with a globally minded, purpose-driven team

Your content reaches nomads and travellers worldwide

Fully remote and flexible schedule

Gain behind-the-scenes insight into a unique international event and community



We are looking for:

A natural communicator with a friendly, professional tone

Organised and consistent - you don't let things slip

Experienced with short-form video editing (Reels, CapCut, Premiere, or similar)

Familiar with Instagram,
, and basic scheduling tools

Comfortable working independently and managing your own time

Proactive - you flag issues and bring ideas without being asked



How to Apply

Please complete our short application form to get started. As part of your application, include a media kit or portfolio with examples of your work - edited videos or Reels, accounts you've managed, or content you've created.

We'll also ask you to record a short 1-minute video telling us what excites you about this role. No need for perfection - we just want to get a sense of who you are.



APPLICATION FORM:

Looking forward to hearing from you,

Emilia

Project & Partnership Manager, Nomad Cruise

title: Growth & Operations Specialist (Content + Outreach)
description:
Role: Growth & Operations Specialist

Type: Performance-Based / High-Growth Role



The Mission

The Tactical Click LLC (TTC) is a premier full stack
 marketing agency. We build revenue infrastructure that turns dormant
 lists into scalable profit engines for brands generating $25,000 to $100,000+ per month.



I am hiring a Specialist work with me, the Founder, to serve as my strategic Right Hand across three core functions: social media content creation and management, lead generation and outreach, and operations and pipeline management. This is a performance-tracked role from day one. If you show up, communicate, and deliver, you will grow fast here.

What You'll Be Doing



Social Media Content Creation and Management



Create original, high authority content across LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and X that positions TTC as the go-to
 marketing agency for B2C and DTC brand owners.



Develop content that educates brand owners on the power of
 marketing, addresses common revenue gaps, and builds trust with founders who are ready to invest in their
 channel.



Manage the full content workflow including content creation, approval submissions, scheduling, and publishing across all platforms.



Monitor engagement, comments, and inbound signals to identify potential leads and warm prospects.



Maintain a consistent posting schedule and content calendar across all platforms.



Lead Generation and Outreach



Identify and research qualified prospects matching TTC's ideal client profile: B2C and DTC brand owners generating $25,000 to $100,000+ per month with an underperforming or dormant
 list.



Execute personalized outreach across all social media platforms with the goal of opening genuine conversations, not pitching services.



Initiate outreach by leading with a specific, credible observation about the prospect's brand that speaks directly to their revenue gap.



Move qualified prospects through the pipeline from first contact to booked discovery call.

Support a target of 8 to 12 qualified discovery calls per month at full capacity.



Pipeline Management and Operations



Maintain accurate and up to date pipeline records in ClickUp so every prospect's status is visible and nothing falls through the cracks.



Provide weekly performance summaries covering content engagement, outreach activity, pipeline status, and any flags that need immediate attention.



Manage the content calendar and ensure all deadlines are met consistently.



Use Google Workspace, ClickUp, and Notion to keep internal operations running cleanly and efficiently.



Working Hours & Requirements

Core Hours: 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM Eastern Time. These are your active working hours. You are expected to be producing and reachable during this window every scheduled day.



Availability Window: 6:00 PM to 10:00 PM Eastern Time. This broader window covers scheduling flexibility, check-ins, and time-sensitive communication.



Commitment: This role is a 10 to 12 hour per week position. During the first month of onboarding hours start at 5 to 6 per week and build by approximately 2 hours per week based on performance and output. Strong performers move through the onboarding period faster and reach full hours sooner. 



This role grows as you grow. The more results you drive for TTC the more hours, responsibility, and compensation progression you can expect.



Onboarding Period: The first 2 to 4 weeks are your foundation window. How fast you move through it depends entirely on your performance and output. Show up, deliver, and communicate and this relationship accelerates.



Tool Stack: Google Workspace, ClickUp, Notion, and a time tracking tool. All working hours are logged and tied to deliverables submitted.



Experience: Previous experience in digital marketing is preferred. Familiarity with
 marketing including
 service providers (ESPs), copywriting, (the power of
 marketing) is a strong plus.



Mindset: Proactive and detail oriented. You identify problems and come with solutions.



How We Operate

TTC runs on clear communication, documented output, and consistent professional standards. Here is what that looks like day to day:



You clock in and clock out via Google Chat at the start and end of every shift or with a time tracking tool.



This is an hourly paid position. You are expected to complete assigned tasks, meet deliverables, and produce measurable results during your scheduled working hours. Strong performance is recognized and rewarded. Rate progression is based on the quality and consistency of your output over time.



All tea
mbers are expected to communicate any scheduling changes or conflicts in advance so workflows stay on track.



Content performance and outreach activity are reviewed weekly so you always know where you stand.



How to Apply (The VELOCITY Filter)

To be considered you must follow these instructions exactly. We prioritize candidates who can execute a brief precisely.



Your application subject line MUST include the word VELOCITY.



Please include the following in your application:



Resume and Portfolio: Show us your best social media content samples and outreach examples. Experience working with B2C or DTC brands is a strong plus.



The Loom: A personalized 2 to 3 minute video explaining your experience in social media content creation, lead generation, or outreach for service based or DTC brands and why you specifically are the right person to represent and work with The Tactical Click.



The Hook Challenge: Take a boring topic such as "Why you need to
 your customers more" and write 3 attention grabbing hooks. One for a LinkedIn post, one for an Instagram Reel caption, and one for a short form X post.



The Outreach Challenge: Write a direct message to a $25k to $100k+/mo brand owner across any platform that opens a genuine conversation about their
 marketing without pitching a service or asking for a call.



The Content Challenge: Most brand owners are sitting on an
 list they have never properly monetized. Write one piece of original social media content for any platform that would stop a founder mid scroll and make them think seriously about what they are leaving on the table.



The Rewrite Challenge:

Improve this: "Hi [Name], I help founders improve their
 revenue. Let me know if you'd like to chat."



Improve this: "Hey, I love your content. Would love to connect."



Compensation: $5 to $9 per hour based on experience and performance. Rate progression is tied to output and results, not tenure.
