title: Manga / Anime Story Writer for Long-Term Black-Inspired Shonen Project
description:
We are looking for a creative writer who loves anime, manga, shonen storytelling, and strong character development to help us grow an original story project over time.



This is a story with big long-term potential. I already have the bare bones of the world, characters, themes, story ideas, visuals, slideshows, and inspiration material, but I need the right writer to help bring it to life in a bigger way.



The goal is to build something that feels inspired by the emotional and epic journey of series like Naruto, One Piece, and Hunter x Hunter, while creating something original that is deeply rooted in Black and African-inspired characters, culture, themes, and perspective. I want this story to feel powerful, meaningful, exciting, and able to get serious when needed, while still having the fun, heart, wonder, and energy people love in shonen manga.



What I need help with:



Expanding my current story ideas into a fuller world

Helping develop arcs, plotlines, lore, and character backstories

Suggesting improvements and fresh ideas that help the story grow

Helping shape pacing, emotional 
nts, rivalries, themes, and long-term payoffs

Organizing story notes and inspiration into a more useful form

Giving feedback on what works and what needs to improve

Possibly helping prepare the story for platforms like WEBTOON, Tapas, MANGA Plus Creators by SHUEISHA, GlobalComix, or other manga/webcomic-friendly platforms

Possibly learning and assisting with AI art generation workflows later on if interested (I can help teach this)



Ideal person:



Loves anime, manga, otaku culture, and storytelling

Understands what makes a great shonen series work

Has respect for and some understanding of Black culture, African-inspired themes, and diverse characters

Can help create something original without making it feel generic

Is imaginative, organized, and good at communication

Is open to a long-term collaboration if we work well together

Can keep story ideas, characters, and project material private and confidential

Is pleasant to work with, humble, creative, and dependable



Nice if you also know about:



WEBTOON

Tapas

MANGA Plus Creators by SHUEISHA

GlobalComix

Webcomic formatting

Manga pacing

Storyboarding or light editing

AI art tools or willingness to learn



Job setup:

9. How soon could you start?

10. Please send samples of your writing or story work or your portfolio link.



We care a lot about communication, trust, creativity, and passion. We are looking for someone who does not just want a random writing job, but someone who would enjoy helping build a meaningful story world over time.

Thank you for your time.

title: Amazon FBA Wholesale Purchasing Manager – Experienced Only
description:
Amazon FBA Wholesale Purchasing Manager – Experienced Only



We are hiring an Amazon FBA Wholesale Account Manager with a strong focus on purchase order management, vendor relationships, and buyer oversight.

This role is heavily focused on reviewing and submitting purchase orders, maintaining communication with vendors, and overseeing a team of buyers by reviewing their work and holding them accountable to ensure accuracy, consistency, and performance.



We’re looking for someone who already has experience managing buyers and reviewing their purchase orders, not someone learning this for the first time.



You will be responsible for managing the day-to-day operations of an Amazon FBA wholesale business. This includes analyzing supplier catalogs, making purchasing decisions, and managing inventory to keep the account running efficiently.

If you’ve actually worked inside a wholesale operation and understand how everything ties together, this will be a strong fit.





Key Responsibilities



Sourcing & Product Analysis

Analyze supplier catalogs to identify profitable wholesale products

Perform deep product analysis using Keepa, Rev Seller, Seller Central data, and other tools

Evaluate competition, buy box behavior, and sales velocity before purchasing



Purchase Orders & Purchasing

Create, review, and approve purchase orders

Ensure pricing, margins, and quantities meet company buying criteria

Submit orders to vendors and track confirmations, invoices, and timelines

Catch and fix mistakes before they impact inventory or cash flow



Vendor Management

Subitting purchase orders and communicating directly with suppliers and distributors

Maintain strong relationships and ensure smooth ordering processes

Handle delays, shortages, pricing issues, and backorders



Buyer Management

Manage and oversee a team of buyers

Review their work and ensure buying decisions meet company buying criteria

Provide feedback, catch mistakes, and enforce consistency

Hold the team accountable for performance and output



Inventory & Replenishment

Track inventory levels and maintain proper stock levels

Plan replenishment based on sales velocity and historical data

Forecast demand and coordinate restocks

Avoid stockouts and over-ordering





Performance Tracking

Track purchasing activity, inventory levels, and overall performance of the buying team

Identify inefficiencies and improve processes where needed. 





Requirements

3+ years of experience managing Amazon FBA wholesale accounts (not private label, not arbitrage)

Strong understanding of Keepa and product analysis

Experience in analyzing supplier catalogs and making purchasing decisions

Experience creating, reviewing, and submitting purchase orders

Experience working directly with suppliers and distributors

Deep familiarity with Seller Central (shipments, reconciliations, account health, troubleshooting)

Understanding of buy box behavior, competition, and replenishment



What We’re Looking For

Detail-oriented — you catch mistakes before they happen

High ownership — you don’t need to be micromanaged

Fast execution — you get things done

Problem solver — you figure things out without constant direction

Clear communicator — direct and organized



Work Schedule

Full-time

Monday to Friday

9AM – 5PM EST

Remote



Compensation

Base salary + performance-based bonus

Paid bi-weekly



Why Join Us

This role offers strong growth potential. As you perform well, you’ll have the opportunity to take on more responsibility and move into a leadership position managing a larger team. You’ll have real ownership over purchasing, vendor relationships, and inventory, with the ability to make an impact on the business. We operate in a fast-moving environment, but with clear systems and structure in place. If you’re looking for a long-term opportunity where you can grow and develop within a wholesale operation, this will be a strong fit.







How to Apply



Include the following:

Summary of your Amazon wholesale experience

Fill out Application: 

Include in application: Loom video explaining your experience (No Loom = automatic rejection)

title: CATSUMO | Short-Form Video Editor & Content VA
description:
CATSUMO | Part-Time Content VA Remote · Part-Time (15–20 hrs/week) · $8–$10/hr USD

?? Start your application with the word SUMO or it won't be reviewed.

About: CATSUMO makes cat toys that bring out your cat's inner wrestler. We're a US-based DTC brand selling on our own site and Amazon, growing fast on TikTok and Instagram. Our content is playful, punchy, and a little absurd — in the best way.

The Role: We're looking for a creative, reliable VA to edit short-form videos, write scroll-stopping copy, and support UGC creator outreach / Product Seeding. Part-time to start, with room to grow.

What You'll Do

Video Editing — primary focus (~65% of role)

Edit short-form vertical videos (TikTok, Instagram Reels) using CapCut or equivalent

Add captions, text overlays, sound effects, trending audio, and transitions

Resize and reformat existing content for different platforms

Produce a minimum of 6–8 polished videos per week from raw footage or UGC clips we provide, with capacity to scale during campaign periods

Maintain consistent brand tone: playful, punchy, never boring

Writing (~15% of role)

Write captions, hooks, and on-screen text that stop the scroll

Adapt copy for TikTok vs. Instagram tone and format

Help draft outreach messages and follow-up scripts for creator contacts

UGC & Product Seeding Outreach (~20% of role)

Research and identify micro-influencers and cat content creators on TikTok and Instagram

Send outreach using scripts and templates we provide, track responses and follow-ups in Google Sheets

Help coordinate product shipment details with interested creators

Spot and flag strong UGC content that could be repurposed for ads or organic posts

---



What We're Looking For



Required:

• At least 1 year of experience editing short-form social media videos professionally

• Proficient in CapCut (desktop or mobile) — other tools like VN, DaVinci, or Premiere are a plus

• Strong eye for pacing, hooks, and what makes a video stop the scroll

• Comfortable writing in a casual, brand-consistent English voice for DMs

• Reliable internet connection and a computer capable of video editing

• Self-starter who can work independently with clear briefs



Nice to Have:

• Experience working with a US-based DTC or ecommerce brand

• Familiarity with TikTok trends, sounds, and the creator ecosystem

• Experience doing influencer outreach or product seeding

• Love of cats is a genuine plus ????



What We're NOT Looking For:

• Beginners or aspiring editors still building their skills — we need someone who can produce independently from day one

• Applicants without a portfolio or work samples

• Anyone who can't commit to consistent weekly availability



Hours & Schedule



• 15–20 hours per week to start, with potential to grow

• Flexible schedule — we care about output, not clock-watching

• Must be available for a weekly 30-minute video check-in (async Loom or live, your choice)

• US Pacific or Mountain time zone overlap preferred (even a few hours)



Rate



$8–$10/hr USD depending on experience and portfolio quality. We pay weekly via Wise or PayPal.



How to Apply

Start your application with the word SUMO — applications that don't include it won't be reviewed.

Stage 1 — Application

Include all of the following:

2–3 short-form videos you've edited (TikTok or Reels style strongly preferred), include download links

A brief note on your CapCut experience and any other editing tools you use

Your available hours per week and time zone

Your desired hourly rate

Portfolio or personal link, if available

Stage 2 — Paid Test Project (shortlisted applicants only)

You'll receive raw footage and a short brief. From that, you'll produce two videos:

One optimized for TikTok inspired by existing content

One optimized for Instagram Reels inspired by existing content

$25 paid upon delivery. 48-hour turnaround required.

Stage 3 — Interview (applicants whose test videos are approved)

A casual 30-minute video call to meet, answer your questions, and confirm fit before we move forward :) 



We respond to every complete application within 3 business days.

mber who can handle both graphic design and social media management tasks. This is a remote, long-term role for someone who is proactive, meets deadlines, and communicates clearly.

/ per hour (Hours depending on project needs) Roughly 20 hours per week



We’re building some of the most recognised brands in the short term rental and accommodation space across Australia and New Zealand. That means conferences, podcasts, digital platforms, websites, content, and a lot of moving parts.



Right now, we’re looking for a self employed contractor who wants more than a standard VA gig. This is a hands on, fast paced support role sitting at the heart of three growing brands:



* STRIVE Conference

Industry leading events and awards bringing together property managers, owners, and suppliers across Australia, New Zealand, and beyond.



* The Accommodation Show

A long running podcast featuring operators, founders, and leaders in hospitality, travel, and short term rentals.



* iBooked.online

A digital agency focused on direct bookings, websites, SEO, and marketing strategy for accommodation businesses.



If you enjoy variety, problem solving, and being close to the action, you’ll feel right at home.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing



This role is deliberately broad. You’ll touch a lot of areas, without being expected to be an expert in all of them from day one.



Admin and operations

* Managing inboxes and basic diary coordination

* Organising data, lists, and spreadsheets

* Supporting day to day business admin across the brands



Content and marketing support

* Scheduling social media posts across multiple platforms

* Assisting with podcast uploads, episode assets, and show notes

* Helping prepare
 campaigns and announcements

* Light content editing and formatting



Web and tech support

* Simple WordPress edits and page updates

* Uploading content, images, and basic changes

* Working with tools like Google Workspace, social schedulers, and
 platforms



Events support

* Helping coordinate speakers, sponsors, and partners

* Keeping track of assets, deadlines, and comms

* Supporting event logistics before, during, and after delivery



You won’t just be following instructions. You’ll be encouraged to spot gaps, suggest improvements, and contribute ideas for content, campaigns, and processes.



Hours, Flexibility & Setup



* Around 20 to 60 hours per week

* Spread across 5 to 6 days

* Fully remote and flexible

* Work to your own rhythm, as long as things get done



This suits freelancers who like consistent work without being locked into rigid hours.



Who This Is Great For



This role is ideal if you:



* Are a freelancer or contractor looking for a long term client

* Enjoy variety rather than doing the same task all day

* Like hospitality, travel, marketing, media, or tech

* Want exposure to events and the short term rental industry

* Are organised, detail oriented, and proactive



You’ll be working with people and brands across Australia and New Zealand, and you’ll build genuine industry connections along the way.



Skills We’re Looking For



* Strong written English

* Confidence with Google Workspace

* Comfortable with social media tools

* Basic WordPress experience

* Good internet connection and a solid home setup

* Curious mindset and willingness to learn



You don’t need to know everything upfront. You do need to be reliable, switched on, and comfortable juggling priorities.



Growth & Pay



As your role expands and your impact grows, your rates will grow too. This is a relationship we want to invest in, not a short term stop gap.



How to Apply



Send us:



* A short intro about you

* A link to something you’ve worked on that you’re proud of

* Your hourly or project based rate



If you like being close to the action and want to grow alongside brands that are genuinely building something in this space, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Virtual Receptionist - Law Office
description:
VIDEO INTRODUCTION REQUIRED



Job Overview:

We are seeking a high-energy, professional, and fluent English-speaking Virtual Receptionist to serve as the first point of contact for our potential new clients. In this role, you aren’t just answering phones—you are the "voice" of our brand. Your goal is to make every caller feel heard, valued, and confident in our services from the very first second.



Key Responsibilities:



Initial Intake: Enthusiastically greet potential clients and gather preliminary information.



Communication: Handle inbound calls and inquiries with a polished, professional, and empathetic tone.



Scheduling: Coordinate and log lead details accurately into our system.



Follow-up: Ensure no potential client is left waiting by managing quick turnarounds on initial inquiries.



Required Qualifications:



Neutral Accent & Fluency: Exceptional verbal and written English skills are non-negotiable.



Personality: You must have a "smile in your voice" and a genuinely helpful, outgoing personality.



Reliability: A quiet home office setup with high-speed internet and a reliable power backup.



Experience: Previous experience in customer service, sales, or front-desk roles is highly preferred.



Why Join Us?

We offer a stable, long-term opportunity with a professional team that values clear communication and hard work.





Please send a brief introduction about yourself and attach a 30-60 second voice recording (or a link to one) telling us why you’re a great fit for a client-facing role. Applications without a voice sample will not be considered.



A Quick Tip for OnlineJobs.ph:

Because "fluency" is subjective on job boards, the voice recording requirement is the best way to filter candidates quickly. You’ll be able to tell within ten seconds if their tone and accent align with your firm's brand.



Would you like me to draft a few specific "screening questions" you can use to further vet the top candidates during the interview phase?

title: IT Operations Support Assistant (2 hrs/week)
description:
Need a reliable, detail-oriented IT freelancer to provide light, ongoing technical operations support for a small technology consulting firm.

Chasm Opportunities LLC needs someone who can be a part-time “IT partner” for a few hours each month. You must be able to work independently with clear written instructions.

Initial Priorities (First 2 Months):

• Migrate existing WordPress site to Hostinger

o Move current WordPress website from a marketing agency’s hosting to Hostinger.

o Ensure all pages, contact forms, and basic tracking continue to work after the move.

o Coordinate with Porkbun for any necessary DNS and
-related changes (domain is managed at Porkbun). 

• Fix and verify Contact form /
 routing

o Confirm that the site’s “Contact Me” form correctly sends
s to my inbox.

o Troubleshoot and resolve any issues so that all form submissions are reliably delivered and not lost or routed to spam.

• Google Workspace admin review (screen-share only)

o Join me on screen-share; you will guide, and I will click.

o Review Google Workspace Business Standard settings for a solo consultant plus freelancers (users, aliases, 2-step verification, sharing defaults, basic security posture).

o Discuss any obvious security gaps or misconfigurations and recommend simple, practical improvements.

• Set up Google Group
 for my small team

o Create one or more Google Groups / group
 addresses that can be read by multiple users (my freelancers and me as needed).

o Help configure delivery, permissions, and access so the group
 works smoothly for the team.

Ongoing Monthly Tasks (2 hours/week total):

• Light WordPress maintenance

o Link new content from my Substack to the website as needed.

o Periodically verify that the contact form is still working.

o Monitor basic tracking (Google tags / analytics and similar tools) and report as needed.

• Basic IT support and troubleshooting

o Help with basic Google Workspace questions, user aliases, groups, and simple configuration changes.

o Assist with small issues related to cloud management (DropBox/OneDrive/Google Drive).

o Provide advice on basic endpoint / PC hygiene when needed.

• RSS and information feeds

o Help build out and maintain a Feedly (or similar) network by adding and organizing RSS feeds relevant to my portfolio of startups (e.g., industry news, grant funding opportunities, government solicitations).

• Basic automation and workflows

o Develop basic automations using Zapier or similar tools, such as:

? Exporting CRM data fields to a spreadsheet.

? Generating auto
s based on web contact form submissions.

? Other simple business automations between cloud tools (e.g., calendar, CRM, forms,
).

• Security awareness and CMMC-aligned support (longer term)

o Have a basic understanding of cybersecurity concepts; familiarity or willingness to learn about CMMC is a plus (no prior CMMC experience required).

o Over time, collaborate with me as I develop a systems security plan to meet emerging CMMC requirements for federal contractors (this will come after higher-priority tasks are completed).

My current tech stack (familiarity is a plus, not essential):

You do not need experience with all of these, but it is a plus if you have worked with some of them or are comfortable learning them:

• Google Workspace Business Standard (primary
/collaboration)

• Porkbun (domain management, DNS,
 hosting) 

• 
 (CRM and work management) 

• Notion (tracking, data storage) 

• Calendly (scheduling) 

• Perplexity Pro (using shared spaces) 

• Dropbox and Slack for client communication and file sharing 

Familiarity with any of these tools is helpful but not required; the core requirements are WordPress, DNS/Google Workspace, CRM experience, RSS management, and basic cloud management (e.g., Dropbox).

Requirements:

• Demonstrated experience with:

o WordPress site administration and migrating WordPress between hosting providers.

o DNS changes for websites and
 (A, CNAME, MX records; basic SPF/DKIM/DMARC understanding is a strong plus).

o Google Workspace Admin console (not just Gmail use) — user management, aliases, basic security settings, groups/distribution lists.

o Managing and organizing RSS feeds (e.g., in Feedly or similar tools).

• Tools and domains:

o Comfort working with CRM tools (any CRM is fine—experience just needs to show you understand basic CRM concepts).

o Experience managing cloud storage tools such as Dropbox or similar. 

o Willingness to work with my specific stack over time (Google Workspace, Porkbun, 
, Notion, Calendly, etc.). 

• Professionalism and trust:

o Ability to work with time-limited access and screensharing (I keep Super Admin and you guide me through changes).

o Reliable for a small but ongoing commitment of 4–6 hours per month.

o Willingness to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) as part of the onboarding process.

• Language:

o English level C1 or C2 (top two tiers on OnlineJobs.ph) is required, to support clear written updates and documentation.

Potential to Grow:

If your service is exceptional and you have the capacity, there is potential to grow into a broader role providing basic IT support for Chasm Opportunities clients as well. This would be a new service offering to my small network of startup companies, where you could help them with light IT, WordPress, Google Workspace, Outlook
, domain management, and similar foundational IT needs.

Work Hours & Logistics:

• Approximately 2 hours per week, ongoing.

• Flexible schedule, but ability to overlap with US Central Time for at least some hours per month for live calls / screen-share sessions.

• Payment via mutually agreed method.

• This role is strictly limited to ~2 hours per week. Tasks will be broken into small, manageable pieces, and I’m comfortable taking longer to complete the overall roadmap.

How to Apply:

Please send a short message that includes:

1. A brief summary of your capability to migrate WordPress sites between hosts.

2. Your experience with Google Workspace Admin (what admin tasks you’ve handled and for what size/type of organization).

3. A short description of your familiarity with DNS and
 authentication (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC).

4. Any experience managing RSS feeds or tools like Feedly (how you organize feeds, and for what purpose).

5. Any experience with tools like Porkbun, 
, Notion, Calendly, Dropbox, or Slack (optional but helpful). 

6. Your typical availability window in Philippines time and your preferred overlap hours with US Central Time.

title: Social Media Content Operator (Video Editor + Content Strategist) – Fitness Brand Scaling to 6 Posts/Day
description:
We are Just Jenn Fitness, an online fitness coaching business helping busy moms lose fat and build confidence.







Our content is direct, relatable to busy moms, and built around real results — not influencer trends.



We are scaling aggressively from 2 posts/day to 4–6 posts/day across Instagram,
, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.



We are looking for someone who can own our content machine from idea ? execution ? publishing.



---



Your Role (Read Carefully):

This is NOT just a video editing job.



You will:



- Build and manage a weekly content calendar

- D
 what content gets posted daily based on what performs

- Turn raw footage into high-performing short-form videos

- Repurpose content across platforms (IG, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, FB)

- Write hooks, captions, and structure for videos

- Schedule and publish content



Jenn (the coach) will:



- Batch record content based on your plan

- Be the face of the brand



You will handle everything else.



---



What Success Looks Like:



- Jenn no longer asks “what should I post?”

- Content is planned at least 7 days in advance

- We consistently post 4–6 optimized videos per day

- Content is improving in views, engagement, and leads



---



Requirements (Non-Negotiable):



- Proven experience with short-form content that performs

- Ability to identify and replicate winning content patterns

- Strong understanding of:

  - Hooks (first 1–3 seconds)

  - Retention and pacing

  - Viral structure

- Experience editing for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Ability to work fast and meet daily deadlines

- Fluent English (for captions, hooks, and messaging)



---



Advanced Skills (Highly Valued):



- Content strategy / content calendar creation

- Copywriting (hooks, captions, CTAs)

- Experience in fitness, weight loss, or coaching niches

- Ability to analyze performance and adjust strategy



---



Workload & Expectations:



- Plan content weekly

- Manage daily posting across platforms

- Edit and deliver multiple videos per day

- Turnaround time: 24 hours or less



---



Compensation:



- Competitive and based on experience

- Higher pay for candidates who can own strategy and execution

- Long-term role with growth potential



---



Application Instructions (READ OR YOU WILL BE IGNORED):



1. Include the word “MOMSTRONG” in your subject line

2. Send 3–5 examples of short-form content you created

3. Answer:

   - How would you plan 7 days of content for a fitness coach targeting busy moms?

   - What are 3 types of videos you would post every week and why?

4. Be willing to complete a paid test project





Final Note:

We are not hiring someone to “help.”



We are hiring someone to take ownership of our content and help us scale.



If you are a creative thinker who understands what makes content perform — and you want to grow with a serious brand — apply.

title: Customer Service Officer
description:
Dear Applicants,



## Customer Service Officer – We're Hiring! ##



We're a fast-growing brand with an engaged, loyal customer base — and as we scale our channels and campaigns, we need someone reliable at the heart of our customer experience. You'll be the person our customers reach when they have questions, the one keeping our records clean, and the engine that keeps our CRM campaigns running on time.



IMPORTANT: If you lack experience, you at least need to have a strong creative instinct and the ability to learn quickly. We value responsible, self-motivated people who take ownership of their work and can deliver quality results with minimal supervision.



Job Scope:

- Handle customer enquiries, feedback, and incoming leads across messaging channels (currently WhatsApp; TikTok to be added)

- Process customer registrations and maintain accurate, up-to-date records

- Monitor and update lead status across the pipeline

- Support CRM campaigns including Holiday programmes, broadcasts, promotions, and follow-up flows



Note:

- Full-time basis – 35 hrs/week (up to 40 hrs during peak periods)

- Monday to Friday: 1pm–8pm (SGT), Saturday: 9am–3pm (SGT) — 6 days per week, each shift inclusive of a 1-hour break

- 14 days of Paid Annual Leave and Paid Public Holidays Benefits (all major holidays in the Philippines) included



IMPORTANT: When applying, please provide:

- Your CV/Resume

- A short video introduction of yourself and why you think you're a good fit

- Why you are leaving your last job or what you were doing just before this application

- Examples of any customer service, admin, or CRM-related work you have done previously. If you do not have prior experience, please be honest and state so — we are open to fast learners with the right attitude



Applications without the above points will be less considered.



P.S. Please format your replies in point form according to the items asked above, e.g.:

My resume is here:

Video introduction here:

I am currently doing... / I am leaving because...

Here are examples of my work...



The more information you provide in your initial application, the better.



Best regards,

Loo Yi

title: Guest support For Short Term Rental Company
description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Guest Support & Operations Analyst to manage overnight guest communications and support key backend operations. This role combines guest relations, reservation management, and administrative tasks to ensure seamless service and operational accuracy.



IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT THIS ROLE

- This is NOT a generic support role.

- You’ll be juggling guests, cleaners, and maintenance teams in real time — often with imperfect information and real consequences if poor decisions are made. Because of that, our hiring process is intentionally more challenging than a typical “message responder” position.

- The assessment you’re about to take is designed to understand how you think — how you prioritize, communicate, and make judgment calls when there isn’t one obvious right answer.

- If you enjoy solving realistic scenarios, thinking critically, and taking ownership, you will likely enjoy both this assessment and the job itself. If you are looking for a simple, scripted support role, this probably won’t be the right fit — and that’s okay.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Guest Communications & Support

- Respond promptly and professionally to booking inquiries, guest messages, and support requests across multiple platforms.

- Provide phone support to address urgent guest needs and ensure timely issue resolution.

- Collaborate with the operations team to facilitate smooth check-ins, check-outs, and overall guest satisfaction.



Operational & Administrative Support

- Perform booking cleanup, ensuring reservations are accurate and conflicts are resolved.

- Conduct guest cleanup by maintaining accurate records and organizing guest profiles.

- Manage listing accuracy and resolve platform-related issues across Airbnb, 
, Expedia, etc.

- Support accounting functions, including expense tracking, invoice updates, and discrepancy reporting.

- Assist in lead management, including organizing, qualifying, and supporting follow-up efforts for potential property acquisition opportunities.



Qualifications

- Experience with short-term rental platforms (Airbnb, 
, Vrbo, etc.).

- Strong communication skills and professional phone etiquette.

- Detail-oriented with strong organizational abilities.

-Tech-savvy and able to work independently during overnight shifts.

- PREFERRED: Experience with PriceLabs, Beds24, accounting tasks, or lead management.



HOW TO APPLY

Upload your resume here >>> 
/s/5sal0p7n

title: Video Editor (Remote)
description:
Job Type: Full-Time or Part-Time

Location: Remote

Salary: Competitive – based on experience



Job Overview



We are looking for 2 creative and trend-savvy YouTube Video Editors to join our team. Your role will be to transform raw footage into engaging, fast-paced, high-impact videos that match current YouTube trends.



If you enjoy storytelling, social media culture, and creating viral-style edits, this role is for you.



Please review these channels to understand the editing style we are looking for:








-









Key Responsibilities:



Video Editing & Post Production

• Edit raw footage into engaging YouTube videos

• Add trendy transitions, sound effects, and motion graphics

• Maintain strong pacing, storytelling, and viewer engagement

• Ensure high-quality audio and video output



Creative Collaboration

• Stay updated with YouTube editing trends and viral styles

• Collaborate with the team to improve video concepts and storytelling

• Suggest editing ideas to improve engagement



Quality & Brand Consistency

• Maintain consistent branding (fonts, style, colors, intro/outro)

• Ensure proper resolution, aspect ratios, and smooth transitions

• Perform final quality checks before publishing



Trend Research

• When editing tasks are light, research new video trends and content ideas

• Participate in weekly or monthly creative meetings



Required Skills:

• 1+ year experience editing YouTube or social media videos

• Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

• Strong understanding of YouTube trends and viral editing styles

• Good storytelling and pacing skills

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

• Reliable internet connection



Preferred (Not Required)

• Basic knowledge of After Effects or motion graphics

• Experience creating short-form content (Reels, Shorts, TikTok)

• Experience designing YouTube thumbnails

• Familiarity with YouTube SEO and analytics

• Experience using Slack, Trello, or similar tools



Work Setup:

• Full-Time: 40 hrs/week | Part-Time: 20–30 hrs/week

• Flexible schedule but must be dependable

• Long-term opportunity



How to Apply:



Please submit:

1. Your resume

2. A portfolio or link to videos you edited

3. Links to YouTube channels or videos you worked on

4. Your expected hourly rate

5. Your weekly availability



TRIAL TASK:

For this Video Editor Trial Task, open the raw footage in the Google Drive folder and create an engaging 30–120 second video using any clips you like. Feel free to add music, captions, or simple transitions to make it more exciting. Once finished, export in MP4 and send the Google Drive link for review.





Join our team and help create engaging videos that captivate millions of viewers. Apply now and be part of our growing content team!



?

title: Social Media Manager
description:
Job Title: Social Media Content Manager

Location: Remote 

Job Type: Full-Time 



About Us:

We are a fast-growing digital creator brand operating multiple channels across YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat, and
. Our content includes family entertainment, challenges, vlogs, skits, pranks, slime content, and lifestyle videos. We are looking for a Social Media Content Manager who can oversee all posting, platform organization, scheduling, and audience engagement across short-form and long-form content.



Position Overview:

The Social Media Content Manager will be responsible for managing our entire social ecosystem. This includes posting, scheduling, optimizing content, writing titles/captions, monitoring trends, engaging with our audience, reminding the team to capture photos/videos, and ensuring consistent daily activity on all platforms. You will work closely with our creators, editors, and content team to keep content flowing smoothly.



Responsibilities:

• Content Posting & Scheduling

• Post all short-form content (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube • • • Shorts,
 Reels, Snapchat Spotlight).

• Post all long-form content on YouTube and
 when needed.

• Upload and schedule posts at the optimal times for each platform.

• Maintain a cross-platform posting calendar.

• Ensure all drafts are properly captioned, titled, tagged, and ready for posting.



Content Management:

• Remind the team daily to take photos, behind-the-scenes, and spontaneous content.

• Organize and manage content folders from editors and creators.

• Track what has been posted, what’s scheduled, and what still needs to be filmed.

• Request additional footage from creators when content gaps appear.



Audience Engagement:

• Respond to comments, messages, and community interactions across all platforms.

• Monitor trending comments or feedback and report insights to the team.

• Encourage audience engagement through questions, CTAs, and community polls.



Optimization & Strategy:

• Research and stay up to date with social media trends, audios, formats, and hashtags.

• Track analytics to determine the best posting times, top-performing content, and weekly growth.

• Write engaging titles, captions, and thumbnail text that improve retention and click-through.

• Identify content opportunities from trends and present weekly ideas to the team.



Brand & Platform Consistency:

• Keep messaging consistent across all accounts.

.

• Review trending audios, 
s, and formats for potential use.

Qualifications:

• 1–3 years of social media experience (creator or influencer marketing preferred).

• Strong understanding of TikTok, YouTube, Instagram,
, and Snapchat algorithms.

• Excellent writing skills (titles, captions, descriptions).

• Ability to multitask and stay extremely organized.

• Knowledge of trending audios, formats, 
 culture, and viral-style editing.





KINDLY DO THE TRIAL TASK:



• Complete a real-world scenario based on supporting Ameerah. The scenario would be busy content week travel, brand deals, and multiple posts due and your task is to create a 1-week organized plan. This should include a content calendar (Tiktok, Youtube Shorts, Instagram), a simple daily schedule for the creator and a checklist of tasks.

title: Drafting and Estimating Administrator
description:
Remote Drafting & Estimating Administrator (Construction / Structural Steel)

Full-Time (40 hrs/week) or Part-Time (20 hrs/week)

Work Schedule: Must be available during Australian business hours

Hours: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Brisbane (QLD) time

(Includes 1-hour unpaid lunch break and two paid 15-minute breaks)



About the Role



We are seeking an experienced Drafting & Estimating Administrator to provide back-end technical and administrative support within the mining and construction industry, specifically in structural steel fabrication and construction.



This role is critical in ensuring accurate technical documentation, cost estimation, compliance alignment, and overall project support.



Role Overview



This is a multi-functional technical and administrative support role. You will work closely with project managers and engineers to ensure project documentation, compliance requirements, and cost estimates are prepared accurately and on time.



1. Compliance Support

Assist with WHS documentation and compliance tracking

Maintain contractor compliance registers

Support project documentation required for mining and construction site entry. Coordinate the creation of MDR (Manufacturer’s Data Record) packages by collating fabrication drawings, ITPs, material traceability records, weld maps, NDT results, and inspection documentation, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with project and client requirements.

Track certifications, licences, and expiry dates



2. Drafting Support

Prepare and revise technical drawings using AutoCAD, Tekla, or similar drafting software

Interpret structural and fabrication drawings

Assist in producing shop drawings and as-built documentation

Maintain drawing registers and version control

Coordinate revisions based on engineering or project changes

The ability to create and work with DXF files and NC1 Files





3. Estimating Support

Assist in preparing cost estimates for structural steel projects

Perform quantity take-offs from drawings

Support material costing, labour costing, and project budgeting

Prepare tender documentation support

Maintain estimating records and pricing databases

4. Administrative, Data Entry & Filing

Maintain structured digital filing systems

Ensure accurate data entry into project management systems

Support invoice tracking and documentation collation

Assist with reporting and documentation preparation

Required Skills & Experience

Technical Skills

Proven experience in drafting or construction drafting

Experience supporting estimating in construction or fabrication projects

Strong proficiency in AutoCAD or similar CAD software

Ability to interpret engineering drawings

Experience working within mining or construction environments 

Administrative & Compliance Skills

Strong document control experience

Familiarity with construction compliance 

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Strong organisational skills

Systems & Software

Microsoft Office Suite and Microsoft 365 Suite

Project management or construction software

Cloud document management platforms

Soft Skills

Strong written communication skills

Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously

Proactive and solution-focused mindset

High level of confidentiality and professionalism





Qualifications

Degree in Drafting, Engineering, Construction Management, or a similar field (preferred)

Minimum 3+ years’ experience in structural steel/ mining/construction support roles

Demonstrated experience in drafting and estimating support

title: Regional Sales Lead – Southeast Asia
description:
Regional Sales Lead – Southeast Asia



Retail & Licensed Consumer Products (D2C Brand Expansion)

Remote | $2,000 USD + Commission





About the Company



We are an 8-figure direct-to-consumer brand operating under a Singapore-based holding company. Our flagship brand generated ~$25M last year and is expanding aggressively into retail channels.



We hold global licenses with major entertainment brands (Disney, Warner, Universal) and are scaling our retail footprint across Southeast Asia.



We are now looking for a commercially sharp sales leader to take ownership of Southeast Asia retail expansion.





The Role



This is not an account servicing role.



You will own retail sales development across Southeast Asia (Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, Singapore), driving licensed product distribution into retail chains and key distributors.



You will build pipeline, structure outreach, close deals, and drive repeat orders.



This role is for someone who thinks in volume, margins, and retail mechanics.





Key Responsibilities



Own retail expansion across Southeast Asia

Build and manage a structured sales pipeline (CRM-driven)

Identify, approach, and close retail chains and key distributors

Follow up on license-driven introductions (Disney, Warner, Universal)

Prioritize direct-to-retail relationships where viable

Develop pricing and volume proposals aligned with margin targets

Conduct presentations and commercial pitches to buying teams

Manage repeat orders and long-term retail partnerships

Coordinate with HQ on product launches and licensing requirements





Product Category



Licensed consumer products including:

Wooden puzzles

Diamond painting kits

3D model kits

Licensed fando
rchandise

Impulse retail items ($2–$5 SKU strategy)



This is a high-volume, price-sensitive retail environment.







What We’re Looking For



Required:



5+ years in B2B sales (retail, FMCG, consumer products, or distribution)

Experience selling into retailers or working with distributors

Proven ability to build pipeline from scratch

Experience handling multi-SKU product catalogs

Strong negotiation and pricing strategy understanding

Comfortable operating without heavy internal systems (startup mindset)

Experience working in Southeast Asia markets





Strong Advantage:



Licensed product experience

Experience with price-sensitive markets

Exposure to major retail chains

CRM implementation experience







Success Metrics (First 6 Months)



Active retail pipeline across 3–5 SEA markets

Distributor engagement strategy defined

Minimum X new retail accounts opened

Repeat orders secured from initial placements





Compensation



$2,000 USD monthly base

Commission on closed deals

High upside based on regional performance

Long-term growth opportunity as SEA division scales







Who Will Thrive Here



Builders, not maintainers

Commercial thinkers

Hunters who can close

Operators comfortable in ambiguity

Sales professionals who understand margin, volume, and positioning







How to Apply

To be considered for the  role:



Complete our application form here:





Submit your updated CV (PDF format preferred).



Answer the 5 screening questions below in your application.



Screening Questions

Have you sold physical consumer products to retailers or distributors? Briefly describe the category and your role.

Describe one B2B deal you personally closed (deal size, sales cycle, and whether you sourced the lead).

Have you opened a new market or territory from scratch? What were your first steps?

Have you negotiated bulk or volume-based pricing? How did you protect margins?

Which Southeast Asia markets have you worked in, and what type of clients did you sell to there?

We are looking for a performance-driven Video Editor who specializes in Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) Meta Ads. This role is focused on creating high-converting video ads, not just visually appealing content.



You must understand direct response marketing, hooks, retention, and conversion psychology—turning raw footage into ads that drive clicks, purchases, and ROI.



Key Responsibilities

Edit high-converting video ads for Meta platforms 
 & Instagram)

Strong portfolio showcasing high-performing Meta Ads 
/Instagram)

Experience editing UGC-style ads, product demos, and testimonial videos

Ability to edit for mobile-first, fast-paced content consumption

Strong sense of timing, pacing, and audience retention techniques



Preferred Qualifications

Experience working with eCommerce or DTC brands

Familiarity with ad metrics (CTR, CPA, ROAS, thumb-stop rate, etc.)

Understanding of consumer psychology and buying triggers

Experience collaborating with media buyers or growth teams

Basic motion graphics and animation skills



What We’re Looking For

A video editor who understands that editing = performance marketing, not just aesthetics

Someone who can think like a marketer, not just execute edits

Highly creative but also data-driven and iterative

Able to quickly produce and test multiple ad variations

Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines

title: Client Account Manager - Direct Hire
description:
Location: Remote

Time-Zone: U.S. Central Time (CST)

Terms: Full-time, Independent contractor

Compensation: $7/hr, negotiable based on experience



ABOUT THE ROLE: 



We are a privately-held overseas staffing startup based in the U.S. We help top U.S. businesses (from celebrity-backed CPG firms to Y Combinator-backed AI startups) hire exceptional remote talent overseas without going through BPOs. We care deeply about providing dignified, redemptive work for people in the Philippines.



We are looking for a Client Account Manager to own the client journey from consultative sales (led by our Founder) through recruitment delivery to onboarding to retention and upsells / overall client success. This role is critical to our growth strategy: allowing our founder to focus on closing new business while you work on delivery + retention. There will be step-up opportunities to define the function, help close deals, and work cross functionally with our awesome teammates. 





ABOUT YOU:  



Are you client-focused, proactive, and process-driven? Do you enjoy building order, systems, and consistency while managing multiple stakeholders? The right person will take ownership of client delivery, anticipate challenges, and maintain high client satisfaction. You will work cross-functionally with Recruiting, Operations, and Finance to ensure a seamless, world-class experience for every client.



RESPONSIBILITIES:  



Client Onboarding & Post-Sales Delivery

- Lead client kick-off calls to align expectations, define roles, and clarify hiring needs.

- Own the client experience immediately after engagement and deposit.

- Communicate the recruitment process step-by-step, including timelines, communication cadence, and deliverables.

- Coordinate with the Recruitment team to ensure sourcing and shortlisting begin promptly.



Recruitment Process Management

- Serve as the main point of contact between clients and internal teams.

- Ensure candidate shortlists are delivered, interviews are scheduled, and feedback is collected timely.

- Follow up persistently with clients to prevent delays while maintaining a professional, friendly tone.

- Coordinate offer extensions, pre-boarding paperwork, and candidate logistics.

- Client Success & Retention



Conduct 30/60/90-day check-ins post-placement to ensure alignment and satisfaction.

- Monitor and escalate client issues before they become critical.

- Identify growth opportunities within accounts, including additional headcount or new roles.

- Oversee smooth onboarding with clients and internal operations (Miya).

- Internal Coordination & Process Improvement



Align daily with Recruitment and Operations teams on client priorities and feedback.

- Maintain accurate records in 
, Workable, and internal CRM/ATS.

- Contribute to refining client-facing SOPs and playbooks.



Requirements

- 3+ years of experience in Client Account Management, Recruitment Account Management, or B2B Customer Success.

- Proven ability to manage multiple clients and processes simultaneously.

- Experience working with U.S. start ups

- Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills (native/bilingual level).

- Comfortable using CRM/ATS/Collaboration tools (Workable, 
, Slack, Zoom/Fathom).

- Ability to work full U.S. Central or Pacific time zones reliably.

- Ability to work reliably and independently in a remote setting with minimal supervision.

- Bachelor Degree or higher (summa, magna or cum laude and/or Academic Scholar from a reputable college preferred) 



Preferred Requirements 

- Experience managing recruitment pipelines in a remote staffing or BPO environment.

- Familiarity with U.S. startup culture, particularly Series B/C companies.

- Bonus: Experience with ATS or CRM integrations, scaling client delivery processes.



To Apply send your resume and a short introduction
 to: 

Subject: Your Name – Applicant for Client Account Manager

Example: Maria Santos – Applicant for Client Account Manager



Please submit your application to the
. DMs are not monitored.

your salary expectation

title: YouTube + Content Growth Virtual Assistant (Remote)
description:
YouTube + Content Growth Virtual Assistant (Remote)

Overview

We’re looking for a detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to support our YouTube channel growth end-to-end: video editing, uploads, SEO optimisation, repurposing into Shorts for multiple platforms, and proactive improvement suggestions.

This role is for someone who understands YouTube as a search and recommendation engine (not just editing), and can spot growth opportunities, content blindspots, and lead-generation angles.



Key Responsibilities

1) YouTube video editing + publishing

Edit long-form videos (clean cuts, pacing, captions/subtitles when needed, simple motion graphics, audio levelling)

Prepare thumbnails (basic design + A/B-ready variations if possible)

Upload and publish videos on schedule

Add end screens, cards, chapters, and pinned comments



2) YouTube SEO + optimisation

Do keyword research and topic validation (search intent + competition)

Optimise titles, descriptions, tags, categories, and hashtags

Recommend playlists and channel structure improvements

Maintain consistent metadata standards across the channel

Track what’s working and iterate (CTR, retention, watch time, traffic sources)



3) Repurposing into Shorts + cross-posting

Create YouTube Shorts from long-form content (hook-first, tight pacing, captions)

Repurpose and format for:

Instagram Reels

TikTok

XHS (Xiaohongshu)

Ensure correct aspect ratios, safe zones, and platform-native best practices



4) Blog + web link maintenance (SEO support)

Maintain blog posts that support video topics (basic formatting + internal linking)

Update web links and embed videos where relevant

Implement simple on-page SEO hygiene (headings, meta title/description drafts, keyword placement, alt text suggestions)



5) Growth monitoring + proactive recommendations

Watch for channel growth opportunities (trending topics, competitor moves, content gaps)

Identify blindspots in content strategy (what we’re not addressing, weak hooks, unclear positioning)

Suggest lead-generation improvements (CTAs, lead magnets, link-in-bio structure, pinned comment strategy)



Required Skills

Proven experience managing and optimising YouTube channels

Strong understanding of:

Keywords, search intent, and YouTube metadata

Playlists, channel organisation, categories, and content positioning

Analytics basics (CTR, retention, AVD, traffic sources)

Video editing skills (any of: CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve)

Shorts editing with strong hooks and pacing

Comfortable repurposing content for IG/TikTok/XHS

Solid written English for titles/descriptions/blog formatting



Nice-to-Have

Thumbnail design skills (Canva/Photoshop)

Basic SEO knowledge for blogs (internal linking, on-page optimisation)

Experience with lead-gen funnels (simple landing pages, lead magnets,
 capture)

Tools You Should Be Comfortable With

YouTube Studio

Video editing software (state what you use)

Video status (editing ? review ? upload ? published)

SEO checklist completion

Shorts created and cross-posted

Notes on performance + next actions



How to Apply (Important)

We are a fast-growing Digital Product Creators company dedicated to helping entrepreneurs turn their knowledge, skills, and ideas into profitable digital products using our all-in-one system powered by Go High Level. We are seeking a driven, energetic, and results-oriented Community Operations Specialist who is passionate about helping people win and thrives in a high-performance, fast-paced environment.



In this role, you will be responsible for onboarding new members, delivering exceptional customer support, hosting trainings and Q&A sessions, and guiding members step-by-step as they build and launch their digital products. You will play a critical role in increasing engagement, retention, and overall community success by conducting outreach, monitoring member progress, celebrating wins, and providing hands-on coaching.



This position also includes managing support communications, assisting with certification programs, leading implementation-based trainings, and supporting revenue growth through recommending and upselling relevant products and services. From time to time, you may also be required to conduct training classes, lead workshops, or step in as a trainer to ensure our community continues to receive high-quality education and support.



We are looking for someone who is confident, proactive, tech-savvy, and able to lead with both strategy and heart—someone who can motivate, educate, and hold members accountable to results. , tickets) Rate 1–10 and give an example.

Also tell us:

title: Credit Repair Specialist / Credit Dispute Expert (Consumer Law)
description:
We’re looking for an experienced Credit Repair Specialist to handle credit disputes and help remove negative items from client reports.



This role requires someone who understands dispute strategies, consumer laws, and how to properly challenge accounts on credit reports.



We are not looking for someone who only sends generic templates — you must understand how to analyze reports and apply the correct dispute strategy.

title: Part-Time Meta Ads Operator
description:
How To Apply



Please reply to this job post with the following:

1. A brief summary of your Meta Ads experience, including typical monthly budgets.

2. Screenshots or summaries of 2 to 3 campaigns you have managed (goal, budget, structure, and results).

3. One paragraph on how you normally set up tracking (pixel, events, CAPI) for a lead gen funnel.

4. Your part-time hourly rate and full-time monthly rate, respectively. 



About Us

We’re a programming education company that helps experienced software engineers build real-world Rust projects and land Rust roles. We run a high-ticket, cohort-based accelerator plus self-paced training, and reach engineers through YouTube,
, and partnerships.



Role Overview

We are looking for a part-time Meta Ads Operator to implement and maintain our
 and Instagram campaigns. This is an execution-focused role: you will take clear targeting and creative direction from the founder and marketing team, then handle setup, tracking, and ongoing optimizations inside Meta Ads Manager.



Ideal for someone who enjoys clean accounts, accurate tracking, and making data-driven tweaks, not sitting in branding meetings.



Key Responsibilities

1. Implement and maintain Meta Pixel and Conversion API across our funnels (waitlist, applications, bookings, purchases).

2. Set up campaigns, ad sets, and ads according to provided specs (objectives, budgets, audiences, and creatives).

3. Create and manage custom and lookalike audiences from our traffic,
 list, and engaged users.

4. Monitor performance daily on weekdays and execute simple rules: pause obvious losers, keep winners within defined guardrails, and flag issues quickly.

5. Run basic A/B tests on creatives and audiences following our testing framework.

6. Ensure tracking events fire correctly on all key steps and troubleshoot when they do not.

7. Produce a concise weekly report (spend, cost per waitlist opt-in, cost per application, cost per call, cost per student) with 2 to 3 clear recommendations.



You Are A Good Fit If

1. You have 1 to 3 years of hands-on experience running Meta Ads (lead gen or info/education experience is a plus).

2. You are comfortable with Meta Ads Manager, Pixel, standard events, and basic CAPI.

3. You can read numbers and make simple performance-based decisions without drama.

4. You are organized, responsive, and document what you do so others can follow it.

5. You can overlap at least 2 to 3 hours per day with US time zones.

6. You’re currently freelancing or working part?time and want a long?term core client, not just a one?off project.



This Role Is Not

1. Not a creative director or copywriter (we have a content and
 team).

2. Not a CMO or growth strategist d
 offers, funnels, or pricing.

3. Not a general social media manager (no organic posting, community management, or influencer work).



Scope & Engagement

1. Part-time contractor.

2. We’ll start at 5–10 hours/week for the first 60–90 days while we validate and scale Meta.

3. We guarantee a minimum of 20 hours/month, paid monthly, so your time is predictable.

4. If performance is strong and we scale ad spend, this role can grow into 15–20+ hours/week and potentially a full-time PH position.

5. Remote, flexible schedule as long as SLAs are met and campaigns are monitored on weekdays.



How To Apply



Please reply to this job post with the following:

1. A brief summary of your Meta Ads experience, including typical monthly budgets.

2. Screenshots or summaries of 2 to 3 campaigns you have managed (goal, budget, structure, and results).

3. One paragraph on how you normally set up tracking (pixel, events, CAPI) for a lead gen funnel.

4. Your part-time hourly rate and full-time monthly rate, respectively.

title: Full-Time Executive Assistant (Growth Operator)
description:
to 50 hours per week in our Startup environment



This is not a typical Executive Assistant role.



--> We are building a consulting company from the ground up and are looking for someone who wants to be part of that process. This role is for someone who takes ownership, moves quickly, and enjoys turning ideas into execution.



--> You will work directly with the Founder to help scale a business that supports dentists through practice acquisition and growth.



What you will be doing

1. Marketing and engagement

You will participate in online groups, forums, and social platforms. This includes posting, responding to comments, and helping drive conversations that generate leads.

2. Lead management

You will handle outreach through text,
, and calls. You will qualify leads, schedule discovery calls, and maintain consistent follow up.

3. Operations and admin

You will build and maintain SOPs and checklists. You will manage inboxes, calendars, and task flow. You will also support onboarding and client communication.

4. Systems and organization

You will keep the CRM clean and up to date, track conversations and leads, and help improve workflows and processes.



What makes you a strong fit

1. You take ownership without needing to be reminded

2. You stay organized while moving quickly

3. You communicate clearly and confidently

4. You adapt easily and figure things out

5. You enjoy both people interaction and behind the scenes execution



Schedule expectations

1. 40 to 50 hours per week

2. Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday

3. Days off are Tuesday and Sunday

4. Typical hours are 10:00 AM to 7:00 PM CST

5. Two 30 minute unpaid breaks each day

6. Payoneer payroll every 2 weeks (note: Recipient pays fees, just like Employees pay taxes in the USA)



--> Saturday availability is required.



Growth opportunity

1. This role can grow into operations, marketing, or project leadership as the company scales. You will be helping build the systems that future tea
mbers will follow.

2. Performance based upside



**There are bonus opportunities tied to lead generation and conversions. Your work directly impacts growth.**



Final note

--> This role is for someone who wants to build something meaningful and take ownership from day one. If that sounds like you, we would like to hear from you.

title: YouTube Social Media Manager & Video Editor
description:
We're a queer family YouTube vlogging channel with 140k subscribers, creating content about LGBTQ+ life, relationships, travel and neurodivergence. We're a two-mom family based in Europe, returning to regular content creation after a break, and we're looking for the right person to help us grow sustainably, particularly with repurposing our long form content to short form and community management (filtering and responding to comments).

We're looking for someone who genuinely connects with what we do and wants to be part of it long term.



What you'll be doing:

Short form content (primary focus)

— Watching our long form YouTube videos and identifying the best 
nts for short form clips

— Editing those clips into vertical format for YouTube Shorts and TikTok

— Adding captions, basic graphics and formatting so they feel native to each platform

note: our audience loves our aesthetic content, so someone with a great eye for visually pleasing edits is important



Community management

— Reading all YouTube and social media comments before they reach us

— Responding warmly to straightforward comments in our voice and tone

— Compiling a weekly digest of meaningful, positive comments for us to read

— Filtering out negative, harmful or unconstructive comments without passing them on to us

— Flagging anything genuinely important that needs our attention



Could also turn into editing our long form content eventually.



Bonus if you can also:

- help with thumbnails and SEO

- give us inspiration for what kind of content to create next, advise on strategy etc.



What we're looking for:

— Strong written English — warm, natural and conversational

— Real understanding of or personal connection to LGBTQ+ community and content — this is important to us, not just a nice to have

— Experience editing short form video content from original horizontal format — please share examples

— Familiarity with TikTok, Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts formats

— Someone who watches YouTube creators for fun and genuinely understands creator culture

— Reliable, proactive, and comfortable working independently

— Someone who wants a long term working relationship, not a short term gig



Practical details:

— Part time, approximately 10-15 hours per week to start

— Fully remote, flexible hours

— Rate: $
/month depending on experience and hours

— Payment via Wise, twice monthly

— Potential to grow the role as we grow



To apply, please send us:

- A few sentences about why this role appeals to you specifically and why we should pick you

- Examples of short form video content you've edited or managed

- Any experience with YouTube or social media community management





Applications without a personal note will not be considered.

title: Video Editor
description:
We are looking for a talented FULL-Time Video Editor to join our team.



Please provide the link to your resume and portfolio when you apply.



**Responsibilities**



-Edit short-form video ads and content for social platforms 
, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)



- Produce engaging clips using hooks, subtitles, transitions, sound design, basic animations and storytelling structure



-Use AI video tools like, Heygen, Veo3 or Higgsfield AI to produce video graphics and accelerate production speed



- Occasionally edit long-form content (10–20 min videos) in a clean, social-optimised style (approx. 1 every 2 weeks)



- Maintain brand consistency and polish across all edits



- Deliver videos ready for publishing on deadline



**Expected Output**



- 3–5 short-form videos per day (30–60 sec each)



- 1 long-form video every 2 weeks, delivered within 2–3 working days



- All edits should reflect modern pacing, formatting, and attention to audience engagement



**Expected Expertise**



Fast and reliable internet



- Proficiency in Final Cut Pro, Adobe Premiere, or similar



- Strong experience editing for social media and video marketing



- Hands-on experience with AI video tools (like Heygen, arcads.ai, Veo3, Higgsfield AI) to enhance output and speed



- Comfortable working with raw footage and turning it into high-performing content



- Working full-time during Australian business hours (Monday to Friday)



- Proven solid work experience of 2+ years as a video editor



Compensation:



- Pay is flexible — per video or fixed monthly rate depending on experience and output



To apply, submit:

- Your resume

- Your portfolio

- A short cover letter explaining why you're the right fit

title: B2B Appointment Setter / Outbound SDR (LinkedIn + Cold Email) | SME & Accounting Firms
description:
I am the founder of Onwards Capita, a fractional CFO and finance execution firm helping SMEs improve cash visibility, working capital discipline, and short-term financial control.

I am hiring one disciplined outbound SDR / appointment setter based in the Philippines to help me generate qualified meetings.

This is not a strategy role.

This is not a closing role.

This is not a content-writing role.

I will provide:

• the target personas

• the outreach strategy

• the message scripts

• the one-pagers / sales material

• the qualification logic



Your role is to execute with discipline:

• identify the right targets

• run outbound on LinkedIn and cold

• follow up consistently

• manage replies and booking flow

• keep the CRM and tracking clean



Scope split

• 80% of your work: outreach to SME founders / decision-makers for a cash control / liquidity offer

• 20% of your work: outreach to accounting firm partners in Singapore and Malaysia for an early-stage pilot offer



What you will do

• Build and verify targeted prospect lists using LinkedIn Sales Navigator and other approved tools

• Identify decision-makers in our target markets and ICPs

• Run structured outbound sequences across:

o LinkedIn connection requests and follow-ups

o cold
 outreach and follow-ups

• Manage responses and keep communication professional, concise and accurate

• Handle basic replies using our pre-approved frameworks

• Escalate nuanced conversations to me

• Push qualified prospects to book a 10–15 minute intro call on my calendar

• Keep records clean in our CRM / tracker

• Report clearly on:

o prospects added

o touches sent

o positive replies

o meetings booked

o meetings held

o pipeline quality



Important

You will not use my personal LinkedIn account.

You will conduct LinkedIn outreach from your own professional profile and use a company
 address for cold
.



Ideal profile

You are a good fit if:

• You have real experience booking B2B meetings through LinkedIn and cold

• Your written English is strong and professional

• You are comfortable reaching out to founders, partners, recruiters, and senior decision-makers

• You know how to research and target properly instead of blasting low-quality lists

• You are organized, responsive, and reliable

• You understand CRM hygiene and follow-up discipline

• You do not need hand-holding every day

Strong advantage if you have

• Experience in B2B SaaS, financial services, consulting, accounting, or high-ticket services

• Experience prospecting Singapore, Malaysia, or US-based businesses

• Experience using LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo, HubSpot, Zoho, or similar tools



Contract structure

• initial 4-week paid test engagement

• 20–25 hours/week

• If performance is strong, this will become a longer-term retainer

Compensation

• Base during 4-week trial: $400–$600/month equivalent, depending on experience, quality, and availability

• Hours: roughly 20–25 hours/week to start

• Bonus: $30 for each qualified meeting held

• Bonus: $200 for each SME client you sourced that signs and pays the first invoice

• Bonus: $30 for each qualified accounting firm partner meeting held

• Bonus: $300 when an accounting firm enters into a formal pilot contract discussion 



What success looks like

In the first 4 weeks, I expect:

• disciplined execution

• strong targeting quality

• clean tracking

• professional communication

• steady follow-up

• qualified meetings, not junk meetings



To apply

Start your application with the words: CASHCONTROL

Then include the following:

1. A short summary of your outbound experience

2. Which tools you have used: Sales Navigator, Apollo, Lemlist, Instantly, HubSpot, Zoho, etc.

3. Two examples of B2B outbound messages you personally sent that led to a booked meeting

4. Which geographies and seniority levels you have prospected before

5. Your availability over the next 4 weeks

6. Your expected monthly compensation for a 20–25 hour/week trial

Low-effort, generic applications will be ignored.

title: Appointment Setter & Sales Support
description:
Do you love speaking with people, creating 
ntum, and opening doors for meaningful business conversations?



Dale Carnegie Australia is looking for a confident, energetic, and proactive Appointment Setter to join our team. This is an outstanding opportunity for someone with a strong phone manner, genuine people skills, and the drive to turn interest into qualified appointments.



In this role, you will be a key part of our growth engine — following up warm leads generated through workshops, events, and marketing activity, building rapport quickly, and booking appointments for our Senior Sales Consultants. You will also help keep our CRM and databases accurate, organised, and up to date.



Reporting directly to the Managing Director, this position suits someone who is resilient, highly motivated, and thrives on 
ntum. If you enjoy making calls, handling conversations with confidence, and staying persistent without losing professionalism, this could be an exceptional fit.



What you’ll be doing:

- Following up leads from workshops, campaigns, and marketing activity

- Calling prospective clients and contacts with confidence and professionalism

- Booking qualified appointments for our Senior Sales Consultants

- Building rapport and creating a strong first impression of Dale Carnegie Australia

- Maintaining accurate notes and updates in our database and CRM

- Supporting the sales process by keeping lead activity organised and moving forward



What we’re looking for:

- A professional and engaging phone manner

- Confidence initiating and leading conversations

- Strong customer service and people skills

- A resilient, positive, and persistent attitude

- High attention to detail and strong follow-through

- Someone who enjoys being part of a team and takes pride in doing great work



What you’ll get in return:

- A supportive team environment where your contribution matters

- A generous bonus structure tied to successful appointments booked

- Ongoing training and development for the right person

- The opportunity to grow with a respected global brand

- A workplace that values loyalty, effort, and long-term contribution



At Dale Carnegie Australia, we genuinely value our people. We are proud to have Virtual Staff who have been with us for more than 5 years — a reflection of the respect, support, and opportunity we aim to provide.



If you are looking for more than just a job, and want to be part of a team where your effort is recognised, your growth is supported, and your work makes a real impact, we would love to hear from you.



How to apply:



Please send us a short video message telling us:

- Why you would like to be considered for this role

- Why you want to join Dale Carnegie Australia

- What makes you a strong fit for this position



We look forward to hearing from you.
